- Lightweight Python agent (< 5MB, < 1% CPU) running in background

- On-device processing: raw screenshots are never transmitted — only structured events

- Captures: application name, window title, UI element interactions (click/type/select), clipboard events, file operations, web page URLs

- Local PII detection and redaction before any data leaves the device

- Captures: process variables (amounts, names, IDs) only in structured format, never raw text

Process Event Stream Processing:

Each desktop event is enriched with context:

- Application mapping: which business system (SAP, Excel, Email, CRM)

- Task inference: clustering similar event sequences into candidate task templates

- User identification (pseudonymized): track which roles perform which processes

Process Discovery Engine:

Sequence mining algorithms (Apriori, SPADE for frequent sequence pattern mining) identify recurring event sequences across multiple users and sessions. Each recurring sequence becomes a candidate process:

1. Process Signature Generation:

Convert event sequences to abstract process signatures that are user-independent (specific values replaced with type placeholders: [AMOUNT], [EMAIL], [DATE])

2. Process Clustering:

DBSCAN clustering of process signatures identifies distinct process variants

3. BPMN Model Generation:

An LLM (GPT-4o) converts structured process signatures into BPMN 2.0 XML notation, generating complete process diagrams with: swim lanes (which role performs which steps), decision points (conditional branches), exception handling paths

4. Process Documentation:

Auto-generates user-friendly process documentation in natural language: "When a new purchase order arrives by email, [user role] opens SAP, navigates to [transaction], enters [order details] from the email, then forwards confirmation to [recipients]."

Automation Code Generation:

For each discovered process, generates production-ready automation code in three formats:

1. Python + PyAutoGUI: For simple desktop automation without licensing cost

2. UiPath XAML: For organizations already using UiPath platform

3. Power Automate flows: For Microsoft 365 environments

Each generated automation includes:

- Input validation and error handling

- Retry logic with exponential backoff

- Logging for audit trails

- Unit tests for all happy path and exception paths

- Business rule documentation as inline comments

ROI Calculation Engine:

For each discovered process: tracks average time per instance, instances per day across all users, computes annual labor cost. When automation is deployed, compares automated vs. manual performance for precise ROI measurement.

title: ChurnPredictor
fullTitle:
ChurnPredictor: AI-Powered Customer Revenue Intelligence and
            Retention Platform
description:
A SaaS platform that integrates with any product
            database, usage analytics, and CRM to build customer-specific churn propensity scores using gradient
            boosting models, identify at-risk customers 60–90 days before they churn, trigger automated personalized
            retention workflows, and calculate the revenue impact of every intervention.

detailedDescription.problem:
Customer churn is the existential threat for every subscription business. A company with 5% monthly churn has a customer lifetime of only 20 months — meaning every customer relationship must pay back its acquisition cost in under two years. This math kills most SaaS businesses before they scale.

The deeply frustrating aspect: most churn is predictable. Research consistently shows that churned customers exhibit behavioral signals 30–90 days before cancellation: declining login frequency, decreased feature usage, reduced team collaboration, failed payment attempts, support ticket volume spikes, or in B2B, executive sponsor departure. These signals exist in every product's database — but no one is watching all of them simultaneously for all customers.

Customer Success teams try to monitor these signals manually, but a 3-person CS team covering 500 accounts cannot track behavioral signals across all accounts simultaneously. They operate on gut feel, focusing on their loudest, not their most at-risk, customers. By the time a customer messages about canceling, the decision is usually already made.

ChurnPredictor automates the signal monitoring, prediction, and intervention pipeline — giving CS teams an early warning system that identifies exactly which customers to call today and what to offer them.
detailedDescription.solution: ChurnPredictor connects to the customer's product database, usage analytics (Mixpanel, Amplitude, Segment), payment processor (Stripe), and CRM (Salesforce, HubSpot) via API. It builds a behavioral feature matrix per customer account, trains a gradient boosting churn model calibrated to the company's specific churn patterns, generates daily risk scores with explanations, and triggers automated personalized outreach workflows for at-risk customers — complete with talking points for CS reps.
detailedDescription.architecture:
Data Integration Layer:

Native connectors for:

- Usage data: Mixpanel, Amplitude, Segment, custom event tracking

- Product database: direct PostgreSQL/MySQL connection for feature usage queries

- Payment: Stripe, Chargebee, Recurly — subscription status, payment history, plan changes

- CRM: Salesforce, HubSpot — contact, account, opportunity, activity data

- Support: Intercom, Zendesk — ticket volume, sentiment, resolution time

- Communication: Slack, Email (last outreach date, engagement)

Feature Engineering:

Automatically computes 150+ churn-predictive features per account:

- Usage features: DAU/WAU/MAU, feature adoption depth, session frequency/length, last login

- Adoption features: % of paid features used, onboarding completion score, integration depth

- Health features: support ticket frequency, sentiment trend, payment failure history

- Relationship features: days since last CS contact, executive sponsor activity, contract age

- Engagement features: email open rate, product announcement clicks, NPS score

- Trend features: 30/60/90-day slope of all usage metrics (declining usage is the strongest signal)

Churn Prediction Model:

XGBoost classifier trained on company's own historical churn data (minimum 200 churned customers for reliable training). Features:

- Automated feature selection using SHAP importance scores

- Class imbalance handling: SMOTE oversampling + class weight adjustment

- Calibrated probability output: confidence score is a true probability, not just a ranking

- Model explainability: every prediction includes the top 5 features driving the risk score

Segmentation and Alerting:

Customers automatically segmented into risk tiers: Critical (>70% 90-day churn probability), High (50–70%), Medium (30–50%), Low (<30%). Daily digest email to CS team: "10 accounts moved to Critical risk today — here are their top risk factors and recommended actions."

Automated Retention Workflows:

Based on account risk tier and identified risk factors, triggers personalized interventions:

- Usage decline: automated in-app contextual tutorial email with specific features the account isn't using

- Low adoption: personal outreach from CS rep with custom ROI calculation ("You're currently using 30% of features; activating X could save your team 5 hours/week")

- Payment failure: automated intelligent dunning sequence (3 increasingly personal emails over 7 days)

- Executive sponsor departure: alert to CS rep with recommendation to initiate executive re-engagement

Revenue Impact Dashboard:

For every intervention: tracks whether the customer churned or retained. Calculates: revenue saved (MRR * contract months remaining), CS team efficiency (accounts managed per rep), churn rate trend, and cohort retention analysis showing how retention improved month-over-month since ChurnPredictor was deployed.

title: PixelForge
fullTitle:
PixelForge: AI Product Photography and Visual Content
            Automation Platform
description:
A platform that takes a single smartphone photo of
            a product and automatically generates unlimited professional e-commerce product images in any environment,
            lighting condition, lifestyle setting, and background — eliminating $2,000–$20,000/day professional
            photography shoots while producing images indistinguishable from studio photography.

detailedDescription.problem:
E-commerce product imagery is the single most important conversion factor for online retail. Studies consistently show that 75% of online shoppers say product photos are the most important factor in purchase decisions. A product with 8+ high-quality images converts at 3x the rate of products with 1–2 images.

Yet professional e-commerce photography is expensive, slow, and inflexible. For a mid-size apparel brand launching a 200-product collection:

- Photography day rate: $2,500–$8,000/day

- Number of days needed: 20–40

- Total photography cost: $50K–$320K

- Timeline from first shoot to live images: 4–8 weeks (including editing, retouching, delivery)

For a brand adding seasonal colors (same product, different colorways), the cost repeats for every color variant. For a brand wanting lifestyle imagery (product in use scenarios), additional shoots are required.

AI image generation has reached a quality threshold where, for many product categories, AI-generated imagery is indistinguishable from photography. PixelForge applies this capability specifically to e-commerce product photography, with the critical addition of consistent product identity preservation across all generated images — ensuring the AI-generated image shows the actual product, not a hallucinated version of it.
detailedDescription.solution: PixelForge takes 3–10 reference photos of the product (standard smartphone photos are sufficient), builds a product-specific LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) fine-tuned model that preserves the product's exact visual identity, and then generates unlimited professional product images in any requested setting. The platform includes 200+ professional photography settings (pure white studio, natural light window, kitchen countertop, outdoor lifestyle, etc.), AI-powered image enhancement (color correction, shadow creation, reflection simulation), and direct export to e-commerce platforms (Shopify, Amazon, WooCommerce).
detailedDescription.architecture:
Core Technical Pipeline:

Phase 1 — Product Identity Learning (Per-Product Fine-Tuning):

Input: 3–10 reference photos of the product

Process:

1. Background removal using SAM (Segment Anything Model) to isolate the product

2. Product feature extraction: color, texture, shape, logo, text elements

3. DreamBooth/LoRA fine-tuning on Stable Diffusion XL base model with the product's reference images

4. Training: ~500–2000 gradient steps (3–8 minutes on A100)

5. Validation: generate test images in standard white background and compare to reference for identity preservation

Output: Product-specific LoRA weights stored per product in the database

Phase 2 — Professional Image Generation:

Input: Product LoRA + scene description (or scene template selection)

Process:

1. Scene preparation: structured text prompt incorporating product LoRA token + scene description from template library

2. Multi-step generation: base SDXL generation → refinement → upscaling (4x with Real-ESRGAN)

3. Composition enforcement: ControlNet pose/depth guidance to place product in natural positions

4. Lighting consistency: BRIA AI background generation with consistent lighting direction

5. Post-processing: automated shadow generation using depth estimation, reflection simulation for applicable surfaces, color profile matching to product reference

Phase 3 — E-Commerce Specific Enhancements:

- Pure white background extraction for marketplace compliance (Amazon, Walmart marketplace requirements)

- Color variant generation: automatically generate images for all color variants without re-shooting ("generate same product in navy blue, forest green, burgundy")

- Scale reference integration: automatically adds scale reference objects (hand holding product, product next to common household items)

- Typography overlay: add product name, feature callouts, promotional text in brand typography

Scene Template Library:

200+ photography-style scene templates organized by product category:

- Apparel: flat lay, wearing (generated model from SDXL), lifestyle scenario

- Food & Beverage: restaurant table, kitchen, natural ingredients styled

- Home Goods: interior room, lifestyle use, close-up texture

- Electronics: desk setup, lifestyle, technical detail shots

- Beauty/Personal Care: vanity setup, ingredient focus, lifestyle application

Platform Interface:

React-based interface with:

- Product library management

- One-click generation from templates

- Custom prompt editor for non-template generations

- Batch generation for multiple products simultaneously

- Side-by-side comparison with reference photos

- Direct Shopify/WooCommerce/Amazon integration for direct publishing

title: MedCode-AI
fullTitle:
MedCode-AI: Autonomous Medical Billing Code Generation and
            Denial Prevention
description:
An NLP system that reads clinical notes, procedure
            reports, and diagnostic findings from EHR systems, autonomously generates optimally coded medical billing
            codes (ICD-10-CM, CPT, HCPCS) with supporting documentation justification, pre-validates claims against
            payer-specific rules to prevent denials, and appeals denied claims using auto-generated supporting
            documentation.

detailedDescription.problem:
Medical coding is the translation of clinical documentation into standardized billing codes (ICD-10-CM for diagnoses, CPT for procedures, HCPCS for supplies and services). It requires coders to read dense clinical notes written in medical jargon, understand the clinical significance of documented findings, select the most specific applicable codes from a taxonomy of 140,000+ ICD-10-CM codes and 10,000+ CPT codes, and ensure the coded claim satisfies payer-specific medical necessity criteria.

The consequences of incorrect coding are severe:

- Upcoding (billing for more than provided): Federal False Claims Act liability, up to $22,000 per false claim

- Downcoding (billing for less than provided): direct revenue loss, averaging $50–$250 per encounter

- Claim denial: 5–10% of claims denied, each costing $25–$117 to reprocess and appeal

- Audit risk: incorrect coding patterns trigger CMS and private payer audits

For a 20-physician practice billing 50 encounters/day per physician (1,000/day total), a 10% error rate means 100 defective claims per day — potentially $5,000–$25,000 in daily revenue impact.

MedCode-AI automates the coding process with accuracy exceeding certified human coders (RHIT) while adding pre-denial validation — a capability no human coder can provide at scale.
detailedDescription.solution: MedCode-AI integrates with EHR systems (Epic, Cerner, Athenahealth, eClinicalWorks) via HL7 FHIR API. When a clinical encounter note is signed, MedCode-AI automatically reads the note, extracts clinical findings (diagnoses, procedures performed, signs/symptoms, risk factors), queries the relevant ICD-10-CM/CPT codebook for the most specific applicable codes, validates the proposed codes against payer-specific LCD (Local Coverage Determination) policies and CMS NCDs, and submits a pre-validated, fully documented claim. Denied claims trigger an appeal workflow using LLM-generated clinical appeal letters citing the specific clinical documentation supporting the billed codes.
detailedDescription.architecture:
Clinical NLP Pipeline:

Clinical Note Understanding:

- EHR integration via HL7 FHIR R4: pulls encounter notes, problem list, medication list, lab results, vital signs

- Clinical NLP preprocessing: sentence segmentation, clinical NER (using SciSpaCy with medical models for diseases, procedures, symptoms, medications, body parts)

- Negation and uncertainty handling: "No chest pain" is not coded as chest pain; "rule out pneumonia" is not coded as pneumonia

- Severity and specificity extraction: "Type 2 diabetes with diabetic CKD stage 3b" maps to a specific ICD-10 code (E11.65), not just "diabetes"

- Temporal relationship extraction: distinguishes current conditions from historical conditions, principal diagnosis from secondary diagnoses

Code Assignment Engine:

ICD-10-CM Coding:

1. Clinical concept normalization using UMLS (Unified Medical Language System) CUI mapping

2. Code hierarchy traversal: start with the most specific applicable code, verify documentation supports that specificity

3. Coding guideline compliance: applies Official Coding Guidelines (OCG) rules automatically

4. Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) optimization: for Medicare Advantage patients, ensures HCC-eligible conditions are coded appropriately for risk adjustment

CPT Coding:

1. Procedure identification from operative notes and procedure reports

2. RVU (Relative Value Unit) optimization: assigns the most specific CPT code that reflects the documented work

3. Modifier application: automatically determines when modifiers (25, 59, TC, 26, etc.) are required

4. Unbundling compliance: flags potentially bundled procedures to avoid compliance risk

Pre-Claim Denial Prevention:

For every proposed claim, runs through a payer-specific rules engine:

- CMS LCD/NCD compliance: verifies diagnosis code supports medical necessity for the billed procedure

- Payer-specific policies: loaded from 150+ major payer policy databases (United, Aetna, BCBS, Cigna, etc.)

- Prior authorization requirements: flags claims requiring PA that wasn't obtained

- Modifier requirement check: identifies when modifiers are required by payer policy

- Documentation requirement verification: ensures the supporting clinical documentation exists in the note

Appeals Automation:

When claims are denied, the system:

1. Classifies denial reason code into category (medical necessity, authorization, coding, documentation)

2. Retrieves the specific payer policy under which the claim was denied

3. Generates a formal appeal letter citing the exact clinical documentation supporting the billed service, referencing the specific payer policy language

4. Submits appeal via payer API or generates formatted letter for mail/fax submission

5. Tracks appeal outcomes for continuous model improvement

title: ClinicalPath
fullTitle:
ClinicalPath: AI-Powered Hospital Readmission Prevention and
            Discharge Planning
description:
A predictive analytics platform integrated with
            hospital EHR systems that identifies patients at high risk of 30-day readmission during their current
            hospitalization, generates personalized evidence-based discharge plans addressing each identified risk
            factor, automatically schedules follow-up care, and coordinates post-discharge monitoring to keep at-risk
            patients out of the hospital.

detailedDescription.problem:
Hospital readmissions represent the most visible and financially consequential quality metric in US healthcare. The Affordable Care Act's Hospital Readmissions Reduction Program (HRRP) penalizes hospitals with excess readmission rates for six target conditions: heart attack, heart failure, pneumonia, COPD, hip/knee replacement, and CABG surgery. Hospitals in the bottom performance quartile can lose up to 3% of all Medicare reimbursements — for a 500-bed hospital, this represents $3–$10M annually.

The clinical problem is real: patients are discharged without adequate support structures, medication reconciliation, or follow-up care coordination. A congestive heart failure patient discharged on Friday afternoon in a hospital without weekend discharge planning services may lack filled prescriptions, scheduled cardiology follow-up, or dietary education — and returns within a week with acute decompensation.

Identifying which patients are at high readmission risk during their hospitalization — before discharge — is the critical intervention window. Current LACE+ and HOSPITAL scores predict only at the point of discharge. ClinicalPath builds longitudinal risk models that update continuously throughout the hospitalization, giving care teams a 48–72 hour window to address risk factors before discharge.
detailedDescription.solution: ClinicalPath integrates with hospital EHR systems via HL7 FHIR to ingest real-time clinical data for all hospitalized patients. A multi-condition readmission risk model (separate models for heart failure, pneumonia, sepsis, surgical, and general medical patients) updates every 4 hours using the patient's evolving clinical picture. For high-risk patients, the system generates an AI-driven discharge planning checklist that directly addresses each identified risk factor, auto-schedules follow-up appointments, coordinates with pharmacy for medication reconciliation, and triggers post-discharge monitoring through patient-facing text reminders and vitals check-ins.
detailedDescription.architecture:
Predictive Model Architecture:

Feature Engineering from EHR:

Extracts 200+ features per patient from HL7 FHIR resources:

- Demographics and socioeconomic: age, insurance type, zip code (mapped to deprivation index), social determinants flags

- Clinical: primary diagnosis, comorbidity count, Charlson Comorbidity Index, vital sign trends, lab value trajectories (sodium, BNP, creatinine trending direction and velocity), medication complexity score

- Hospitalization characteristics: admit type, length of stay so far, ICU time, procedure count, consult frequency

- Care process features: weekend admission flag, resident-only coverage, discharge planning initiation timing

- Prior utilization: ED visits in past 6 months, prior readmissions, prior hospitalizations

Multi-Condition Risk Models:

Separate gradient boosting models (LightGBM) trained per condition:

- Heart Failure model: 200K+ HF hospitalizations from CMS HRRP public data + institution-specific data

- Features: BNP trajectory, diuretic response, weight trend, discharge weight vs. dry weight

- Sepsis/infection model: PCT trend, antibiotic escalation, culture results pending

- Surgical model: wound classification, surgeon-specific readmission rates, post-op complication flags

- General medical model: ensemble of LACE+, HOSPITAL score, and custom features

Each model provides:

- 30-day readmission probability (calibrated, not just ranked)

- Risk tier assignment (Critical >35%, High 20-35%, Medium 10-20%, Standard <10%)< /p>

- SHAP feature importance explaining the top 5 risk drivers for this patient

Discharge Planning Intelligence:

For high-risk patients, generates a structured Discharge Risk Reduction Plan:

1. Medication Reconciliation Checklist:

Identifies high-risk medication combinations, missed reconciliation with pre-admission medications, medications newly started during hospitalization that require follow-up monitoring

2. Follow-Up Scheduling:

Based on risk factors and discharge diagnosis, recommends:

- Cardiology follow-up within 7 days for HF patients (auto-schedules via EHR)

- Lab monitoring orders placed at time of discharge (e.g., potassium check in 3 days for patients on diuretics)

- SNF (Skilled Nursing Facility) placement for patients with functional decline

3. Patient Education Personalization:

Based on identified risk factors, selects and personalizes patient education materials:

- Identified risk: "Patient lives alone, has no transportation" → enhanced social work referral + telehealth plan

- Identified risk: "Previous non-adherence to medications" → pharmacy counseling + pill organizer order

Post-Discharge Monitoring (Remote Patient Monitoring):

For highest-risk patients (>35% probability), deploys post-discharge monitoring:

- Daily text message symptom check (automated NLP response classification)

- Bluetooth scale/BP monitor data capture for cardiac patients

- Automated escalation: if weight increases >2 lbs in 24h, alerts care coordinator for proactive call

title: FraudNeuron
fullTitle:
FraudNeuron: Real-Time Transaction Fraud Detection Neural
            Network Platform
description:
A real-time payment fraud detection platform using
            an ensemble of Graph Neural Networks, LSTM sequence models, and XGBoost classifiers that detects fraud
            across card-present, card-not-present, ACH, and wire transfer transactions with sub-100ms latency, sub-0.1%
            false positive rate, and continuous online learning from confirmed fraud feedback — deployed as a scoring
            API that integrates into any payment processor or bank's authorization pipeline.

detailedDescription.problem:
Payment fraud is an arms race between fraudsters continuously improving their tactics and fraud detection systems that must adapt in real-time. The challenge is multidimensional:

1. Extreme class imbalance: fraud represents 0.1–0.5% of all transactions. Simple models achieve 99.5%+ accuracy by predicting "not fraud" for everything — while missing all actual fraud.

2. Speed requirement: fraud scoring must complete in under 100ms to fit within the payment authorization window. Complex ensemble models must be engineered for sub-millisecond inference.

3. False positive cost: a 1% false positive rate means 1 in 100 legitimate transactions is declined. For a bank processing 1M transactions/day, that's 10,000 valid transactions declined — costing the bank $50K–$200K in merchant fees and causing significant customer experience damage.

4. Concept drift: fraud patterns change weekly as fraudsters adapt to detection. Models deployed in January need continuous updates by March to maintain performance.

5. Graph structure: the most sophisticated fraud detection requires understanding relationship networks — a single fraudster controls 500 synthetic identities connected through shared device fingerprints, email domains, and payment patterns. Only graph-based models can detect these coordinated attacks.

FraudNeuron addresses all five challenges simultaneously with an architecture no individual bank can build in isolation.
detailedDescription.solution: FraudNeuron is a three-layer fraud detection ensemble. Layer 1: a Graph Neural Network (GNN) processes the transaction graph (connections between accounts, devices, IP addresses, merchants) to detect coordinated fraud rings. Layer 2: an LSTM sequence model analyzes the cardholder's transaction history for behavioral anomalies. Layer 3: XGBoost on 200+ engineered features provides the primary high-speed scoring. The three layers are combined by a stacking model that weights each layer's output based on transaction type and available context. Online learning pipelines update all models daily using confirmed fraud labels from chargeback data.
detailedDescription.architecture:
Three-Layer Detection Architecture:

Layer 1 — Graph Neural Network (Fraud Ring Detection):

Constructs a heterogeneous transaction graph with multiple node types:

- Account nodes: bank accounts, card numbers (hashed)

- Entity nodes: devices (fingerprints), IP addresses, email addresses, physical addresses, phone numbers

- Merchant nodes: merchant IDs, merchant category codes

Edge types:

- transaction: account → merchant

- uses_device: account → device

- shares_address: account ↔ account (via shared address)

- email_domain: account → email domain

GraphSAGE (graph sample and aggregate) model learns embeddings for each node type. During inference, the account's neighborhood embedding captures its relationships to other nodes — critical for detecting synthetic identity fraud rings where 50+ fake identities share device fingerprints.

Updated daily with confirmed fraud labels. Inference: account embedding retrieval from pre-computed embedding store + subgraph aggregation takes <5ms.< /p>

Layer 2 — Behavioral LSTM (Anomaly Detection):

Maintains a rolling 90-day transaction history per cardholder. LSTM sequence model trained to predict "normal" next transaction given history. At inference time, computes anomaly score as log-probability of the current transaction given history. High anomaly = low log-probability.

Features encoded per transaction in sequence: merchant category, amount (log-scaled), hour of day, day of week, geolocation (encoded as geohash), channel (online/in-store/ATM).

Trained on 500M+ historical transactions. Handles cold-start for new accounts using population-based priors by card product and customer demographics.

Layer 3 — XGBoost Ensemble (Primary Scorer):

200+ engineered features, split into categories:

- Transaction features: amount vs. account average, merchant risk score, BIN (Bank Identification Number) risk score, MCC (Merchant Category Code) risk, currency mismatch

- Velocity features: transactions in last 1h/6h/24h/7d, amount in last windows, unique merchants in windows

- Device features: device age, device fraud history, VPN/Tor flag, browser inconsistency flags

- Behavioral features: hour-of-day normality, geo-velocity (impossible travel), channel switching

- Contextual features: time since last declined transaction, previous chargebacks

Trained with weighted logistic loss to handle class imbalance. Calibrated output for interpretable probability scores.

Meta-Stacker:

Combines GNN embedding, LSTM anomaly score, and XGBoost probability into a final fraud score using a logistic meta-learner. Meta-stacker is transaction-type specific (separate models for CNP online, CP in-store, ACH, wire).

Online Learning Pipeline:

Chargeback data (confirmed fraud labels) received daily → triggers model feature engineering for affected transactions → appends to training set → triggers scheduled retraining (weekly full retrain, daily incremental update via streaming gradient descent for XGBoost). Model performance metrics monitored continuously; auto-rollback if new model degrades.

Deployment Architecture:

FastAPI inference service deployed as Docker containers with horizontal scaling. Redis for account embedding cache and velocity counters. Apache Kafka for high-throughput transaction stream ingestion. PostgreSQL for transaction storage and feature logging.

API:

Single /score endpoint: accepts transaction JSON, returns fraud_score (0-1), risk_tier, top_risk_factors, recommended_action (approve/review/decline) within <100ms.< /p>

title: ModelGuard
fullTitle:
ModelGuard: ML Model Observability, Drift Detection, and
            Auto-Retraining Platform
description:
A comprehensive ML model monitoring platform that
            continuously tracks model performance in production — detecting feature drift, prediction drift, and data
            quality degradation — generates automated root cause analyses for performance degradation, triggers
            configurable auto-retraining pipelines, and provides a single observability dashboard for all models across
            an organization's ML portfolio.

detailedDescription.problem:
ML model deployment is only the beginning of the operational challenge. In production, the real world changes continuously:

- Feature drift: the statistical distribution of input features shifts (e.g., customer age distribution changes as target demographics evolve)

- Concept drift: the relationship between features and the target variable changes (e.g., economic conditions change which features predict loan default)

- Data quality: upstream data pipelines develop errors (null values, format changes, schema drift)

- Label drift: the definition of what's being predicted changes (e.g., "churn" is redefined)

Any of these causes model performance to degrade silently. The business impact is direct and often large:

- A recommendation model drifting from 12% to 8% CTR costs an e-commerce platform 33% of recommendation revenue

- A pricing model drifting from 95% to 85% accuracy costs a subscription business 10% margin per customer

Yet 67% of companies have no systematic monitoring. They discover degraded models through downstream business metric drops — at which point significant damage has already occurred.

ModelGuard provides the production observability layer that the ML ecosystem has been missing.
detailedDescription.solution: ModelGuard integrates into any ML deployment pipeline via SDK or proxy wrapper. For each deployed model, it logs prediction inputs and outputs, computes drift metrics continuously using statistical tests (PSI, KL divergence, Kolmogorov-Smirnov, Jensen-Shannon), monitors labeled feedback to compute live accuracy metrics, and generates automated root cause analysis reports when degradation is detected. Auto-retraining pipelines trigger on configurable drift thresholds.
detailedDescription.architecture:
Model Monitoring Architecture:

Data Collection via SDK Wrapper:

Python SDK wraps any model's predict() call — one-line integration:

```python

from modelguard import ModelGuardWrapper

model = ModelGuardWrapper(your_model, model_id="fraud_v3", project="payments")

prediction = model.predict(features) # Automatically logged

```

For each prediction, logs: timestamp, model_id, model_version, input features (schema-validated), prediction output, prediction probability/score, and any available ground truth label (via async label ingestion API).

Statistical Drift Detection Engine:

For each input feature, computes drift metrics continuously:

- PSI (Population Stability Index): classical actuarial measure, PSI > 0.25 = significant drift

- KS test: Kolmogorov-Smirnov test for distributional difference between baseline and current

- JS divergence: Jensen-Shannon divergence for categorical features

- Wasserstein distance: earth mover's distance for continuous features

For model outputs:

- Prediction distribution drift: has the proportion of positive/negative predictions changed?

- Calibration drift: are predicted probabilities still calibrated against actual outcomes?

- Feature importance stability: has which features drive predictions changed?

For data quality:

- Missing value rates per feature (alerting when rate increases)

- Cardinality monitoring for categorical features

- Range violations: numerical features exceeding historical min/max

Automated Root Cause Analysis:

When drift alerts trigger, the RCA Agent:

1. Identifies which features are drifting most significantly

2. Segments analysis: is drift happening for all predictions or a specific subpopulation?

3. Correlates feature drift with upstream data pipeline changes (if CI/CD integration is present)

4. Generates a natural language RCA report: "Model performance dropped 6% between Nov 15–Dec 1. Root cause: Feature 'credit_utilization' distribution shifted significantly, with mean increasing from 0.43 to 0.67. This coincides with a data pipeline change on Nov 14 that affected the credit utilization calculation logic. Recommend: validate pipeline calculation and retrain model on recent data."

Auto-Retraining Pipeline:

Configurable triggers (drift thresholds, performance thresholds, time-based) kick off automated retraining:

1. Fetch training data from configured data source (feature store, data warehouse)

2. Apply configured preprocessing pipeline

3. Train using original training script (stored in model registry)

4. Evaluate new model on recent labeled data

5. If performance improves, automatically deploy to staging for human approval (or auto-deploy if configured)

6. Canary deployment: route 5% traffic to new model, compare metrics, roll out fully if acceptable

Multi-Model Dashboard:

React dashboard showing: all models across the organization, each model's performance over time, active alerts, drift heatmap, retraining job status.

title: PredictMaint
fullTitle:
PredictMaint: Industrial Equipment Failure Prediction and
            Maintenance Optimization
description:
An industrial IoT + ML platform that ingests sensor
            data from manufacturing equipment (vibration, temperature, current draw, acoustic emissions), detects
            anomalous patterns indicating impending failure 2–8 weeks before breakdowns, calculates optimal maintenance
            windows, and auto-generates maintenance work orders — shifting industrial maintenance from
            reactive/scheduled to predictive and outcome-optimal.

detailedDescription.problem:
Industrial maintenance operates in one of two modes, both suboptimal:

Reactive maintenance (run-to-failure): The cheapest approach operationally, but catastrophic when failures cause unplanned downtime. A failed pump in a chemical plant can shut an entire production line for 4–8 hours while replacement parts are sourced. Direct downtime cost: $20K–$500K depending on the process.

Preventive maintenance (calendar-based): Replace bearings every 3,000 hours, regardless of actual condition. Studies show 30–50% of preventive maintenance tasks are performed on equipment that would have continued operating well beyond the service interval. Meanwhile, the other 50–70% fail before the interval is reached because calendar schedules don't account for operating condition variations.

Predictive maintenance (condition-based) is the theoretically optimal solution — monitor actual equipment health and maintain only when needed, with enough warning to plan maintenance during scheduled production windows. The challenge: implementing predictive maintenance requires:

1. Sensor instrumentation of every critical machine (IIoT sensors)

2. A data pipeline handling sensor data from hundreds of machines

3. Domain-specific ML models trained on failure mode data that often doesn't exist in labeled form

4. Integration with CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management Systems) for work order generation

PredictMaint provides this complete stack, with pre-trained anomaly detection models that work even without labeled failure data.
detailedDescription.solution: PredictMaint deploys IIoT sensor nodes on critical equipment (vibration, temperature, current, acoustic emission sensors) that stream data to a cloud platform at 1kHz–10kHz sampling rates. An edge preprocessing layer performs feature extraction (FFT for vibration spectral analysis, statistical features) before cloud transmission to manage bandwidth. The cloud ML platform runs multi-modal anomaly detection: Isolation Forest for statistical outliers, LSTM autoencoders for temporal pattern anomalies, and physics-informed models for specific failure modes (bearing defect frequency analysis). When anomaly confidence exceeds threshold, generates maintenance work orders with calculated urgency scores, estimated remaining useful life, and recommended maintenance actions based on the anomaly signature.
detailedDescription.architecture:
Technical Architecture:

Edge Layer — Sensor Data Collection and Preprocessing:

IIoT Hardware:

- Vibration: MEMS accelerometer triaxial sensors (±16g, sampling at 3.2kHz)

- Temperature: PT100 resistance temperature detectors with 0.1°C accuracy

- Current draw: hall-effect current sensors for each motor phase

- Acoustic emission: piezoelectric sensors 100kHz–1MHz for crack propagation detection

- Optional: oil particle counters for lubrication analysis

Edge Computing (Raspberry Pi CM4 or NVIDIA Jetson Nano):

Runs real-time feature extraction before cloud transmission:

- Time domain features: RMS, peak, kurtosis, crest factor, skewness (computed per 1-second window)

- Frequency domain features: FFT power spectral density, bearing defect characteristic frequencies (BPFI, BPFO, BSF, FTF extracted from bearing geometry data)

- Wavelet features: discrete wavelet transform coefficients for transient detection

Transmits feature vectors (not raw sensor data) to cloud at 1 vector/second, reducing bandwidth by 3000:1

Cloud ML Platform:

Layer 1 — Statistical Anomaly Detection:

Isolation Forest trained on 30 days of normal operation baseline. Automatically re-baselines when extended normal operation is recorded (handles seasonal operational differences). Detects unusual readings but doesn't diagnose failure mode.

Layer 2 — LSTM Autoencoder (Temporal Anomaly):

Trained to reconstruct normal temporal patterns of sensor readings. At inference, reconstruction error is the anomaly score — if current patterns are unlike anything in normal operation history, reconstruction error spikes. Particularly effective at detecting gradual degradation trends that point measurements miss.

Layer 3 — Physics-Informed Failure Mode Models:

For common failure modes with known physics:

- Bearing defects: automatic extraction of BPFI/BPFO frequencies from vibration spectrum, comparison against theoretical defect frequencies

- Imbalance: sub-synchronous vibration at 1x rotation frequency

- Misalignment: vibration at 2x rotation frequency with axial component

- Looseness: vibration at sub-harmonic frequencies

Each detected failure mode provides a specific diagnosis ("outer race bearing defect at 3.2x the normal level").

Remaining Useful Life (RUL) Prediction:

For equipment with identified degradation patterns, a regression model (trained on similar equipment failure histories from public NASA CMAPSS and IEEE PHM data + institution-specific data) estimates remaining useful life with confidence intervals.

CMMS Integration:

Integrates with SAP PM, IBM Maximo, Infor EAM, UpKeep via REST API. When anomaly score exceeds configured threshold:

1. Auto-generates work order with: equipment ID, anomaly description, failure mode diagnosis, estimated RUL, recommended maintenance action, required parts (retrieved from equipment Bill of Materials), and suggested maintenance window (avoiding scheduled production windows)

2. Assigns work order to maintenance crew based on skill requirements

3. Flags required parts for pre-ordering if RUL > 2 weeks

Plant Dashboard:

Real-time plant map showing equipment health status (green/yellow/red), active anomalies, upcoming maintenance schedule, and maintenance cost savings vs. previous reactive approach.

title: PatentCraft
fullTitle:
PatentCraft: AI Patent Claim Drafting and Prior Art Analysis
            Platform
description:
An AI platform that analyzes a technical invention
            disclosure, automatically drafts independent and dependent patent claims in proper patent claim syntax,
            searches 150M+ patents for prior art and freedom-to-operate analysis, identifies the claim scope that
            maximizes protection while avoiding prior art, and drafts a complete patent application — reducing
            first-draft patent preparation from 20+ attorney hours to 2 hours.

detailedDescription.problem:
The US patent system processes 650,000+ patent applications annually, yet the $15,000–$20,000 average cost of a utility patent application creates a significant access barrier. Innovative small companies and solo inventors generate 30%+ of US patent applications but represent 60–70% of those who decline to file due to cost. The economic loss from foregone patent protection at companies that can't afford it is immeasurable in terms of unprotected innovations.

On the attorney side, the economics are also inefficient. A patent attorney's highest-value work is claim strategy: determining which aspects of an invention are patentable, how broad to claim them, and how to differentiate from the prior art landscape. Yet 60–70% of their billing hours are consumed by:

1. Prior art searching: manually searching Google Patents, USPTO, EPO for relevant prior art

2. Claim drafting: converting technical disclosures into the rigid formal language of patent claims

3. Specification drafting: writing the detailed description supporting the claims

4. Format compliance: ensuring the application meets USPTO/EPO formal requirements

PatentCraft automates the mechanical aspects of patent prosecution while enhancing the strategic aspects that require legal judgment.
detailedDescription.solution: PatentCraft takes an invention disclosure (technical description, drawings if available) as input. It automatically extracts the novel technical features, classifies the invention into CPC/IPC patent classification codes, searches 150M+ global patents for prior art using semantic patent search, identifies the defensible claim scope (what's patentable vs. anticipated by prior art), drafts a complete set of patent claims (independent + dependent) in proper USPTO claim syntax, and generates a supporting specification. The attorney reviews and adjusts the AI-generated first draft — transforming their role from drafter to strategist.
detailedDescription.architecture:
Technical Architecture:

Invention Understanding Engine:

1. Technical feature extraction: NLP pipeline extracts the key technical elements of the invention (components, relationships, functions, properties)

2. Novelty hypothesis generation: identifies which technical combinations appear to be novel vs. conventional

3. Patent classification: assigns CPC (Cooperative Patent Classification) and IPC codes using a trained classifier (fine-tuned on 2M+ patent classification pairs)

4. Inventive concept mapping: generates a structured representation of the invention's technical contributions

Prior Art Search Engine:

Multi-source, multi-strategy search:

1. Semantic patent search: generates dense embeddings for the invention using PatentBERT (BERT fine-tuned on patent text) and searches patent vector indexes

- USPTO PatentsView API (9M+ US patents)

- EPO Open Patent Services (150M+ global patents in Espacenet)

- Google Patents API

2. Citation network analysis: identifies highly cited patents in the relevant space

3. Non-patent literature: searches arXiv, PubMed, ACM Digital Library for technical prior art

4. Results ranking: reranks retrieved patents by relevance using cross-encoder model

5. Claim mapping: for the most relevant prior art, performs automated claim element mapping ("Does this reference disclose the claimed [specific element]?")

Freedom-to-Operate Analysis:

For each relevant prior art patent found:

1. Independent claim extraction and parsing

2. Claim element mapping against the proposed invention

3. Claim scope assessment: likely invalid/valid assessment with reasoning

4. Infringement risk scoring: probability that practicing the invention infringes existing patents

Claim Drafting Engine:

Trained on 500,000+ patent claims in a fine-tuned LLM:

1. Claim architecture planning: determines optimal independent/dependent claim structure for maximum protection

2. Independent claim drafting: generates 3+ independent claims with varying scope (broad, medium, narrow) to give strategic flexibility

3. Dependent claim generation: generates 20–30 dependent claims covering specific embodiments and fallback positions

4. Claim language compliance: validates claim language against USPTO claim drafting guidelines (no "I," "we," "the invention," means-plus-function triggers)

5. Claim differentiation check: verifies each claim is distinguishable from prior art

Specification Generation:

- Abstract: 150-word summary in USPTO format

- Background: problem statement with cited prior art

- Summary: summary of the invention and claims

- Detailed description: expanded technical description enabling a POSITA (Person of Ordinary Skill in the Art) to practice the invention

- Claims: formatted claim set

- Drawings descriptions: figure descriptions if drawings uploaded

Output: Complete patent application in USPTO EFS-Web compatible format (PDF + XML)

title: RouteGenius
fullTitle:
RouteGenius: AI Fleet Route Optimization and Dynamic Dispatch
            Engine
description:
A real-time fleet routing optimization platform
            using a hybrid metaheuristic + deep reinforcement learning approach that solves Vehicle Routing Problems
            (VRP) with time windows, capacity constraints, and dynamic events (traffic, driver breaks, order
            cancellations, new orders) for fleets of 50–5,000 vehicles — reducing total distance driven by 15–30%, fuel
            costs by 12–25%, and delivery time windows by 40%.

detailedDescription.problem:
Route optimization is one of the most economically impactful applications of combinatorial optimization. For a delivery company with 100 drivers making 20 stops each, the difference between a good route and the optimal route can be 15–25 km/driver/day — multiplied by 100 drivers, 250 working days, and $0.50/km cost, that's $187,500–$312,500 in annual savings from a single optimization improvement.

The mathematical challenge: the Vehicle Routing Problem with Time Windows (VRPTW) is NP-hard. For a fleet of 100 vehicles and 2,000 delivery locations, brute-force optimization is computationally impossible. Commercial solvers (Google OR-Tools, CPLEX) work well for static scenarios but struggle with the dynamic reality of fleet operations:

- New orders received after routes are dispatched

- Driver breakdown requiring route redistribution

- Traffic accident blocking a key corridor

- Customer request to change delivery time window

Current solutions either optimize routes statically (ignoring dynamics) or use simple greedy heuristics for dynamic events. RouteGenius uses a deep RL policy trained on millions of routing scenarios that can re-optimize routes in real-time for dynamic events in under 1 second.
detailedDescription.solution: RouteGenius combines Google OR-Tools for initial static route planning with a deep reinforcement learning policy (Attention Model / Pointer Network architecture) for dynamic re-optimization. The RL policy is pre-trained on 10M+ VRP scenarios and can respond to route disruptions by re-optimizing affected vehicles' routes in <1 second — fast enough for real-time dynamic dispatch. Integration with live traffic data (Google Maps Platform / HERE Maps) and delivery management systems provides the full operational platform.
detailedDescription.architecture:
Optimization Architecture:

Static Route Planning (Initial Optimization):

- OR-Tools with LKH-3 (Lin-Kernighan Helsgott) heuristic for high-quality initial solutions

- Handles VRP variants: VRPTW (time windows), CVRP (capacity), VRPPD (pickup and delivery), multi-depot

- Solution quality: within 2–5% of optimal for problems of practical size (1,000+ stops)

- Computation time: <30 seconds for 500 stops, <120 seconds for 2,000 stops

Dynamic Re-optimization (RL Policy):

Architecture: Attention Model (AM) with multi-head attention + Pointer Network

- State encoding: current route state for each vehicle (position, remaining capacity, remaining time window slack), remaining unserved stops

- Action: assign next stop to a vehicle or wait

- Reward: minimize total route cost (distance + time + time window violation penalty)

- Training: REINFORCE with baseline, trained on 10M synthetic VRP instances with random dynamic events

At inference time (when a dynamic event occurs):

1. Receive event (new order, cancellation, traffic, vehicle breakdown)

2. Update route state representation

3. RL policy generates re-optimized route assignments in 100–500ms

4. Dispatcher confirms automatically or reviews high-impact changes

Traffic Integration:

- Real-time traffic via Google Maps Platform Traffic API (or HERE Maps)

- Dynamic estimated travel time updates every 5 minutes for all active route segments

- Predictive traffic: uses time-of-day traffic patterns to estimate future congestion on planned routes

- Automatic rerouting when estimated traffic delay exceeds configured threshold

Driver App (React Native):

- Turn-by-turn navigation with optimized route

- Delivery status updates (arrived, delivered, failed) that trigger automatic downstream adjustments

- Electronic proof of delivery (photo + signature)

- Real-time chat with dispatcher

- Break management: requests for driver breaks trigger route re-optimization

Fleet Analytics Dashboard:

- Real-time fleet map with all vehicles and their routes

- KPI dashboard: stops/hour, on-time delivery rate, distance per stop, fuel efficiency

- Historical performance analysis: route efficiency over time, driver performance comparison

- Cost breakdown: fuel, labor, vehicle maintenance allocation per route

title: DemandPulse
fullTitle:
DemandPulse: AI-Powered Demand Forecasting and Inventory
            Optimization Platform
description:
A multi-variate demand forecasting platform that
            ingests sales history, external signals (weather, events, competitor pricing, economic indicators, social
            media trends), and generates probabilistic demand forecasts per SKU per location, then automatically
            calculates optimal inventory levels, reorder points, and safety stock — reducing overstock by 20–35% and
            stockouts by 40–60%.

detailedDescription.problem:
Inventory optimization is one of the most complex operational problems in retail and manufacturing. The challenge is not just predicting future demand (hard enough), but translating probabilistic demand forecasts into inventory decisions that balance:

- Stock availability: customers expect products to be available (stockouts destroy customer loyalty)

- Capital efficiency: excess inventory ties up working capital and risks markdown/spoilage

- Supply chain lead times: orders must be placed weeks/months in advance

- Demand uncertainty: forecasts are imperfect; safety stock buffers the uncertainty

- SKU proliferation: a mid-size retailer may have 50,000+ SKUs across 100+ locations

Traditional approaches fail because:

1. Statistical forecasting models (ARIMA, Holt-Winters) capture seasonal patterns but miss external drivers (a cold snap doubles demand for soup, a social media trend doubles demand for a specific product, a competitor stockout creates demand spillover)

2. Inventory optimization models use single-point demand estimates instead of probability distributions — resulting in systematically incorrect safety stock calculations

3. Models are built once and not updated as demand patterns evolve

DemandPulse addresses all three with an ML-based probabilistic forecasting engine that continuously learns from new data and incorporates 50+ external demand signals.
detailedDescription.solution: DemandPulse ingests historical sales data, inventory records, and promotional calendars from ERP/POS systems, then augments with 50+ external signal APIs (weather, events, social media, competitor pricing, economic indicators). A hierarchical forecasting model (N-BEATS at the SKU level, LightGBM with external features at the category level) generates probabilistic demand forecasts (5th, 25th, 50th, 75th, 95th percentile) per SKU per location for the next 12 weeks. An inventory optimization engine converts these probabilistic forecasts into optimal inventory parameters: reorder point, order quantity, safety stock — customized per SKU's lead time, demand variability, and profit margin.
detailedDescription.architecture:
Forecasting Engine Architecture:

Data Integration:

- Sales/POS: Shopify, WooCommerce, SAP, Oracle Retail — historical daily sales per SKU per location

- Inventory: current stock levels, on-order quantities, supplier lead times

- Promotional calendar: planned promotions, events, markdowns

- Weather API: OpenWeatherMap forecasts for location-specific weather effects

- Events API: local event calendars, holidays, school schedules

- Economic indicators: consumer confidence index, inflation data, employment

- Social media trends: Google Trends API for product category interest

- Competitor pricing: web scraping (with caching) for competitor price comparisons

Feature Engineering:

Per SKU per location per day:

- Lag features: sales at t-7, t-14, t-28, t-365

- Rolling statistics: 7/14/28/91-day mean, std, max sales

- Calendar features: day of week, week of year, days to/from major holidays, payday effects

- Promotional features: is_on_promotion, discount_depth, promotion_type

- External features: temperature, precipitation, local event flag, Google Trends score

- Product features: category, price point, new/established product lifecycle

Hierarchical Forecasting:

Top level: total category demand (high signal-to-noise) — LightGBM with external features

Bottom level: individual SKU demand — N-BEATS neural network capturing complex temporal patterns

Reconciliation: MinT (Minimum Trace) hierarchical reconciliation ensures top-down consistency

Probabilistic Output:

Instead of point forecasts, generates demand probability distribution per period using quantile regression + conformal prediction intervals. This enables proper inventory optimization accounting for forecast uncertainty.

Inventory Optimization Engine:

Given probabilistic demand forecasts and supply parameters:

- Service level targeting: calculates safety stock for configurable service level (e.g., 95% in-stock)

- Reorder point: demand during lead time at target service level

- Order quantity: EOQ (Economic Order Quantity) modified for minimum order quantities

- Dynamic optimization: updates parameters weekly as forecasts update

- Markdown recommendations: flags items where excess inventory requires promotional action

Automatic Replenishment:

Integrates with ERP purchasing modules to automatically generate purchase orders when inventory hits reorder point, subject to configurable human approval thresholds.

SKU-Level Analytics Dashboard:

For each SKU: forecast vs. actual chart, inventory position over time, stockout events, overstock events, revenue impact calculation.

title: ESGLens
fullTitle:
ESGLens: Corporate ESG Score Generation and Climate Risk
            Quantification Engine
description:
Real-time, independent ESG scoring platform using
            satellite imagery, regulatory databases, NLP news analysis, and supply chain data to generate transparent,
            weekly-updated ESG scores with full methodology documentation.

detailedDescription.problem: The ESG data market is $1.3B growing at 25% CAGR, yet the underlying data quality problem is widely acknowledged. A 2019 MIT study found that ESG ratings from different providers correlate at only 0.61 on average — meaning investors using different providers are making systematically different investment decisions for the same companies. ESGLens builds real-time, forward-looking ESG scores from independent data sources.
detailedDescription.solution: ESGLens ingests 15+ independent data sources: satellite imagery for environmental monitoring (methane plumes, deforestation), regulatory violation databases (EPA ECHO, OSHA), carbon emission estimation models, news NLP sentiment, CDP supply chain disclosures, patent filings for green technology, and Scope 1/2/3 emission estimates. A GNN models supply chain relationships to propagate ESG risk scores upstream through the value chain.
detailedDescription.architecture: Environmental Scoring: Satellite imagery analysis from Sentinel-5P for methane plume detection, Planet Labs for deforestation monitoring, physical emissions factor model calibrated against validated Scope 1/2 data. Regulatory violation database integration: EPA ECHO, OSHA violations, EU ETS compliance. Social Scoring: NLP analysis of news coverage for labor practices, supply chain transparency scores from CDP. Governance Scoring: SEC filing analysis for board independence, executive compensation structure, related-party transactions. Forward-looking Risk: Regulatory analysis pipeline monitors upcoming climate regulation in all major jurisdictions and scores each company's transition risk exposure.

title: GrantHunter
fullTitle:
GrantHunter: AI Research Funding Discovery and Proposal
            Generation Platform
description:
Continuous monitoring of 500+ government and
            private grant databases with semantic matching and automated first-draft grant proposal generation tailored
            to each funding opportunity.

detailedDescription.problem: The academic funding landscape is simultaneously vast and nearly inaccessible. The NIH alone announces 1,000+ new funding opportunities per year. NSF has 500+ active programs. Researchers at well-resourced universities employ dedicated grant writers; researchers at smaller institutions lack this infrastructure. GrantHunter democratizes access by automating discovery and first-draft proposal generation.
detailedDescription.solution: GrantHunter maintains a continuously updated database of all open grant opportunities scraped from Grants.gov, NIH Guide, NSF website, and private foundation portals. A semantic matching engine identifies relevant opportunities monthly. For each high-match opportunity, a proposal generation agent reads the full FOA, extracts evaluation criteria and priority areas, and generates a customized first-draft proposal incorporating the researcher's prior publications and preliminary data.
detailedDescription.architecture: Grants Database Construction: Daily scraping of 500+ funding sources with FOA parsing: eligibility, deadline, budget parameters, review criteria extracted into structured schemas. Researcher Profile Matching: Bidirectional BERT encoder model computing semantic similarity between researcher profiles and grant descriptions. Proposal Generation Pipeline: FOA analysis → researcher context assembly → specific aims generation (fine-tuned on 500+ funded NIH specific aims) → significance section → research strategy → bibliography via Zotero/Mendeley integration. Budget Justification: Standard NIH/NSF rate table integration for personnel, indirect costs, and common budget categories.

title: TalentGraph
fullTitle:
TalentGraph: AI-Powered Skills Intelligence and Workforce
            Planning Platform
description:
Enterprise workforce intelligence platform building
            a skills graph from HRIS, performance reviews, project records, and LinkedIn profiles to map skill gaps,
            identify internal mobility opportunities, and generate personalized learning paths.

detailedDescription.problem: Enterprise HR sits on an enormous skills intelligence problem: data about every employee's job history, performance, projects, and accomplishments spread across HRIS systems, performance management platforms, project trackers, and LMS systems — but no unified skills understanding. TalentGraph provides real-time skills intelligence that makes internal talent visible, comparable, and actionable.
detailedDescription.solution: TalentGraph ingests employee data from HRIS (Workday, SAP SuccessFactors), performance management platforms, LMS completion records, and with employee consent, LinkedIn profiles. An NLP pipeline extracts skills using ESCO taxonomy. A knowledge graph models skill relationships and career transition pathways. A workforce planning module compares current skills against projected future needs.
detailedDescription.architecture: Skills Extraction: Multi-label model fine-tuned on O*NET/ESCO skills taxonomy. Performance review NLP extracts demonstrated behavioral competencies. Project metadata maps technologies to skills. LinkedIn OAuth integration for verified endorsements. Graph Construction (Neo4j): Nodes: employees and skills. Edges: has_skill (proficiency weighted), worked_with, managed_by, similar_career_path_to. Workforce Planning: Business leaders define future capability requirements; platform calculates internal development potential, hiring requirement, and reskilling risk. Skills Marketplace: Internal platform matching employees to open roles, mentorship connections, and learning resources.

title: StructureAI
fullTitle:
StructureAI: Automated Engineering Drawing Analysis and
            Structural Compliance Platform
description:
AI system reading engineering drawings to extract
            structural elements, auto-generate FEA models, run structural simulations for multiple load cases, and
            produce IBC/ASCE 7 code compliance reports — compressing weeks of manual structural review to hours.

detailedDescription.problem: The AEC industry has seen negative productivity growth since 1945. Structural engineering review is manually performed by licensed engineers reviewing 50-500 pages of drawings, cross-referencing specifications, and manually checking each connection against code requirements. StructureAI automates mechanical review aspects while elevating the engineer's role to reviewing AI-generated analysis.
detailedDescription.solution: StructureAI takes structural drawings (PDF or DXF/DWG) as input. Computer vision extracts structural elements using LayoutLM-based document understanding. NLP extracts specifications, materials, and loads. OpenSeesPy FEA model auto-generated. Multiple load cases analyzed. IBC/ASCE 7 compliance checked. Results compiled into compliance report with pass/fail per check and code section references.
detailedDescription.architecture: Drawing Understanding: LayoutLM fine-tuned on 50,000+ structural drawing pages. Identifies beams, columns, walls, slabs, connections, section markers. Section schedule parsing extracts beam/column sizes. Specification parsing extracts concrete strength, steel grade. FEA Model Generation: OpenSeesPy model from extracted geometry and section properties. ASCE 7 load pattern application. Mesh generation for slab elements. Analysis and Code Checking: Linear static, modal, and ELF analyses. Stress ratios (Mu/ϕMn, Vu/ϕVn), deflection limits, connection capacity. Compliance report with code section references.

title: AdaptLearn
fullTitle:
AdaptLearn: AI Adaptive Learning Platform with Bayesian
            Knowledge Mastery Tracking
description:
Enterprise corporate learning platform using
            Bayesian Knowledge Tracing to identify each learner's precise knowledge gaps, dynamically generating
            personalized micro-learning content targeting those gaps with on-demand GPT-4o generation, and producing
            quantified skill mastery scores mapping to job performance metrics.

detailedDescription.problem: Corporate training fails not because content is bad but because it's not delivered at the right time, to the right person, at the right depth. Knowledge acquisition follows well-established cognitive science principles (spaced repetition, interleaving, retrieval practice) that traditional LMS platforms systematically ignore. AdaptLearn applies these principles at scale with AI-generated content that adapts in real-time to each learner's demonstrated knowledge state.
detailedDescription.solution: AdaptLearn uses Bayesian Knowledge Tracing (BKT) to track each learner's mastery probability per concept in a knowledge graph. A content generation engine creates personalized practice questions, explanations, and examples targeting the learner's current knowledge frontier. Content generated on-demand by GPT-4o with learner context and domain corpus as grounding. Spaced repetition scheduling prevents forgetting.
detailedDescription.architecture: Bayesian Knowledge Tracing: Per (learner, concept) pair: P(mastery), P(transit), P(slip), P(guess). Updates on every interaction via Bayes theorem. Deep Knowledge Tracing (DKT) LSTM model for learners with sufficient history. Content Generation: For each learning session, identifies target concepts (highest learning benefit given current mastery). Generates adaptive content: targeted explanation, worked examples for learner's role, 3-5 practice questions of calibrated difficulty, elaborative interrogation prompts. All content validated against domain corpus for factual accuracy. Performance Correlation: Integrates with performance management and sales systems. Regression model correlating knowledge mastery scores with job performance metrics — creating the training ROI measurement that L&D departments need.

title: ContractSig
fullTitle:
ContractSig: Blockchain-Anchored Smart Contract Execution
            Platform for B2B Agreements
description:
B2B contract execution platform converting standard
            commercial agreements into self-executing smart contracts where payment terms, performance milestones, and
            penalty clauses are automatically enforced based on verified oracle data inputs, eliminating disputes about
            contract performance.

detailedDescription.problem: Commercial contracts are legally binding but operationally brittle. An SLA between a SaaS vendor and enterprise client specifying '99.9% uptime or 5% credit' immediately leads to disputes when outages occur. ContractSig provides the bridge: attorneys draft contracts in natural language, the system identifies automatable clauses, translates them into verifiable conditions, and deploys hybrid contracts on Hyperledger Fabric where objective performance enforcement is automatic while traditional legal remedies remain available for subjective disputes.
detailedDescription.solution: Contract drafting interface where attorneys create commercial agreements. NLP engine identifies automatable clauses. Smart contract conditions translated with oracle connections. Deployed on Hyperledger Besu (permissioned, private). When oracle confirms triggering condition, smart contract automatically executes agreed action. All actions recorded in legal evidence trail.
detailedDescription.architecture: Contract Intelligence Layer: NLP pipeline identifies automatable clauses: payment triggers, performance metrics, milestone payments. For each clause, proposes oracle connection: SLA data from Datadog/PagerDuty, delivery confirmation from logistics APIs, project milestones from Jira/GitHub. Smart Contract Generation: Solidity on Hyperledger Besu. State machine for contract lifecycle; event triggers mapped to oracle inputs; automatic payment via Stripe API; dispute escalation pathway. Legal Integration: All smart contract actions recorded in legal evidence trail with timestamped blockchain records, oracle data logs, and execution certificates. Payment Execution: Stripe Connect for direct settlement, ACH for bank transfer.

title: AuditBrain
fullTitle:
AuditBrain: AI Financial Audit Evidence and 100% Transaction
            Anomaly Detection
description:
An AI audit platform that ingests complete general
            ledger data and applies ML anomaly detection across 100% of transactions—vs the 2-5% sample in traditional
            audits—identifying journal entry irregularities, related-party transactions, unusual period-end entries, and
            authorization anomalies, generating evidence-ready work papers that cut audit fieldwork by 60%.

detailedDescription.problem: Public company audits cost $1.5M–$15M annually; 60-70% of audit time is consumed by manual evidence collection, sampling, and workpaper preparation. Traditional sampling misses fraud hidden in the un-sampled 95-98% of transactions. Financial statement fraud costs investors $800B annually; 80% of fraud schemes involve journal entry manipulation that complete-population testing would catch.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI audit platform that ingests complete general ledger data and applies ML anomaly detection across 100% of transactions—vs the 2-5% sample in traditional audits—identifying journal entry irregularities, related-party transactions, unusual period-end entries, and authorization anomalies, generating evidence-ready work papers that cut audit fieldwork by 60%.
detailedDescription.architecture: GL Ingestion: accepts trial balance exports from SAP, Oracle, QuickBooks, Sage, NetSuite via CSV/API. Complete-population journal entry testing using Benford's Law analysis (first-digit distribution anomalies), round-number transaction detection, weekend/holiday posting flags, same-day reversal detection, duplicate transaction clustering, and unusual account-to-account relationship mining via graph analysis. ML anomaly scoring: Isolation Forest on transaction feature vectors including: amount, posting date, preparer ID, approver ID, account combination, description text embedding. NLP analysis of journal entry descriptions detects vague/unusual language patterns correlated with manipulative entries. Related-party transaction detection uses entity resolution across vendor master, employee, and board/executive records. Work paper auto-generation: for each flagged anomaly, generates an audit work paper with: test objective, population description, exception details, risk assessment, and recommended follow-up procedures in PCAOB/ISA compliant format.

title: CropOracle
fullTitle:
CropOracle: Multi-Spectral Agricultural Yield Prediction and
            Precision Farming Platform
description:
A precision agriculture platform that fuses drone
            multispectral imagery, satellite data, weather forecasts, soil sensor readings, and historical yield records
            to generate field-level crop yield predictions with 92%+ accuracy 8 weeks before harvest, and then generates
            precision input prescriptions (fertilizer, irrigation, pesticide) that maximize yield while minimizing input
            costs.

detailedDescription.problem: US farmers lose $20B annually to yield variability from preventable causes: nutrient deficiencies, pest/disease outbreaks, water stress. Traditional soil sampling costs $300-500/field and is done every 3-5 years. Uniform input application wastes 30-40% of fertilizer and pesticides applied in areas that don't need them, costing $50B globally in unnecessary agricultural inputs annually.
detailedDescription.solution: A precision agriculture platform that fuses drone multispectral imagery, satellite data, weather forecasts, soil sensor readings, and historical yield records to generate field-level crop yield predictions with 92%+ accuracy 8 weeks before harvest, and then generates precision input prescriptions (fertilizer, irrigation, pesticide) that maximize yield while minimizing input costs.
detailedDescription.architecture: Data Fusion Architecture: Sentinel-2 satellite imagery (10m resolution, 5-day revisit) processed for NDVI, NDRE, NDWI, SAVI vegetation indices. Drone imagery at 5-10cm resolution for sub-field variability mapping. IoT soil sensors (moisture, EC, temperature at 6-inch and 18-inch depths). Weather station data + NWS/ECMWF 14-day forecast integration. Historical yield maps from combine GPS yield monitors. Yield Prediction Model: CNN-LSTM hybrid architecture processing satellite time-series (weekly NDVI curves through the growing season). Gradient boosting ensemble (XGBoost + LightGBM) with 150+ features: weather stress indices, growing degree days, soil parameter z-scores, management history. Calibrated against 5 years of field-level yield records. Output: yield prediction map at 10m resolution with 80/90/95th percentile confidence intervals. Variable Rate Prescription Generation: Zone delineation from unsupervised clustering (k-means on composite feature raster). Per-zone input optimization using crop response curves calibrated to local soil/climate. Outputs: variable rate fertilizer application maps (N, P, K rate per zone), irrigation scheduling per zone, pesticide application targeting (only spray zones above disease/pest threshold). Compatible with John Deere Operations Center, Climate FieldView, and CNH AFS for direct prescription upload to equipment.

title: PolicyBot
fullTitle:
PolicyBot: Insurance Underwriting Automation and Risk Pricing
            Engine
description:
An AI underwriting platform for commercial
            insurance lines (property, casualty, professional liability, cyber) that ingests application data, public
            records, satellite imagery, news coverage, and financial disclosures to generate risk scores, recommend
            coverage terms, and produce binding premium quotes in minutes—replacing 2-4 week manual underwriting
            workflows and reducing loss ratios by 8-15 percentage points.

detailedDescription.problem: Commercial insurance underwriting takes 2-4 weeks for complex risks, consuming 60% of underwriter time on data collection rather than judgment. Insurance carriers pay $30B annually in claims that were foreseeable from better risk selection. InsurTech MGAs with automated underwriting are capturing market share from traditional carriers at 3x the speed and 30% lower combined ratios.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI underwriting platform for commercial insurance lines (property, casualty, professional liability, cyber) that ingests application data, public records, satellite imagery, news coverage, and financial disclosures to generate risk scores, recommend coverage terms, and produce binding premium quotes in minutes—replacing 2-4 week manual underwriting workflows and reducing loss ratios by 8-15 percentage points.
detailedDescription.architecture: Data enrichment pipeline: ACORD application data ingestion → automated enrichment from 40+ data sources: D&B financials, SIC code industry loss statistics, state court records (prior litigation), OSHA violation records, EPA enforcement, BBB complaint data, social media adverse coverage, satellite property imagery (condition assessment via CV model), website technology stack analysis (for cyber liability). Risk scoring model: Gradient boosting ensemble trained on 10 years of loss data per coverage line. Separate models per line of business: GL, property, cyber, E&O, D&O. Features: company financials, industry risk factors, operational characteristics, management team background scores, location risk (crime, weather, flood zone). Model outputs calibrated probability of loss, expected loss severity, and combined ratio projection. Pricing engine: actuarial pricing model using GLM base rate adjusted by ML risk score. Deviation analysis: flags accounts where ML score deviates significantly from traditional actuarial pricing for senior review. Terms generation: automatically generates coverage form, endorsements, exclusions, and premium based on risk profile. Submission to quote in <15 minutes for 80% of accounts.

title: ResumeX
fullTitle:
ResumeX: AI Technical Hiring Assessment and Anti-Gaming
            Interview Platform
description:
A technical hiring platform that generates
            role-specific, AI-resistant coding and system design assessments, proctors them using behavioral biometrics
            (typing cadence, gaze tracking, copy-paste detection), evaluates submissions with semantic code analysis
            beyond just test-passing, and generates structured interview guides with targeted follow-up questions based
            on each candidate's specific gaps—cutting time-to-hire by 60% and increasing quality-of-hire by 40%.

detailedDescription.problem: Technical hiring costs $28,000 per software engineer hire on average; 40% of engineering hires are mismatched within 90 days. LeetCode-style assessments are gamed by 60%+ of candidates using AI code generators; companies either trust compromised assessments or spend $3,000–$8,000 per candidate on live technical interviews that don't scale. Unstructured interviews introduce 45% bias variance into hiring decisions.
detailedDescription.solution: A technical hiring platform that generates role-specific, AI-resistant coding and system design assessments, proctors them using behavioral biometrics (typing cadence, gaze tracking, copy-paste detection), evaluates submissions with semantic code analysis beyond just test-passing, and generates structured interview guides with targeted follow-up questions based on each candidate's specific gaps—cutting time-to-hire by 60% and increasing quality-of-hire by 40%.
