detailedDescription.architecture: Assessment Generation Engine: For each role, generates novel assessment problems that cannot be solved by standard AI tools because they're specific to the company's actual codebase, tech stack, and problem domain. Problem generation uses GPT-4o with a custom prompt that creates problems requiring: (1) understanding of the specific technology ecosystem (unusual framework combinations, version-specific behavior), (2) domain knowledge the role requires (e.g., financial calculations for fintech roles), (3) debugging of deliberately subtle bugs in realistic code. Anti-Gaming Layer: Behavioral biometrics collect typing cadence patterns, mouse movement entropy, paste events, window focus changes, and code writing velocity. An anomaly detection model compares submission behavior against a baseline of authentic coding behavior. Gaze tracking via webcam (MediaPipe Face Mesh) detects external reference consulting. Code semantic analysis: beyond test case passing—evaluates code style consistency with stated experience level, uses of obscure optimizations inconsistent with typical knowledge, and temporal consistency of code complexity. Evaluation Model: multi-dimensional scoring using static analysis (code quality, patterns, naming), execution analysis (correctness, edge cases, performance), semantic analysis (approach quality, idiomatic language use), and behavioral integrity score. Outputs a structured hiring brief with: technical capability rating per skill area, integrity score, specific knowledge gaps identified, and 15 targeted follow-up interview questions generated from the specific weaknesses observed.

title: CarbonChain
fullTitle:
CarbonChain: Corporate Supply Chain Carbon Accounting and Scope
            3 Automation
description:
An automated Scope 3 carbon accounting platform
            that integrates with procurement, logistics, and financial systems to trace emissions through multi-tier
            supply chains, automatically calculates emissions using spend-based and activity-based methods, identifies
            the 20% of suppliers causing 80% of emissions, and generates CDP/GRI/TCFD-compliant carbon
            disclosures—replacing 6-month manual Scope 3 assessments with continuous real-time accounting.

detailedDescription.problem: Scope 3 emissions represent 70-90% of a company's total carbon footprint but are tracked by fewer than 15% of companies because manual supply chain carbon accounting requires 6-12 months of consulting work costing $200K-$1M. SEC's new climate disclosure rule and EU CSRD require Scope 3 reporting from 2025; companies face $50K-$1M regulatory penalties for non-compliance.
detailedDescription.solution: An automated Scope 3 carbon accounting platform that integrates with procurement, logistics, and financial systems to trace emissions through multi-tier supply chains, automatically calculates emissions using spend-based and activity-based methods, identifies the 20% of suppliers causing 80% of emissions, and generates CDP/GRI/TCFD-compliant carbon disclosures—replacing 6-month manual Scope 3 assessments with continuous real-time accounting.
detailedDescription.architecture: Scope 3 Category Coverage: All 15 GHG Protocol Scope 3 categories automated: purchased goods/services (Category 1), capital goods (Category 2), fuel/energy activities (Category 3), upstream transportation (Category 4), waste (Category 5), business travel (Category 6), employee commuting (Category 7), upstream leased assets (Category 8), downstream transportation (Category 9), processing of sold products (Category 10), use of sold products (Category 11), end-of-life treatment (Category 12), downstream leased assets (Category 13), franchises (Category 14), investments (Category 15). Emissions Calculation Methods: Spend-based: purchase amount × industry-average emissions factor (EPA EEIO database, Exiobase MRIO model). Activity-based: actual quantity × activity-specific emission factor (ecoinvent database 3.9 with 18,000+ factors). Supplier-specific: primary data from supplier ESG disclosures. Automatic method selection based on data availability, with uncertainty quantification per category. Supply chain graph analysis: multi-tier supplier network built from procurement data + D&B supply chain intelligence. Graph algorithms identify critical emission hotspots. Reduction opportunity identification: benchmarks each supplier against sector peers, identifies top-10 highest-emission reduction opportunities with estimated abatement cost ($/tCO2e). Disclosure automation: generates CDP questionnaire responses, GRI 305 disclosures, TCFD transition risk sections, and SEC climate disclosure draft sections. Continuous accounting: monthly emission updates as new procurement data flows in—no more annual point-in-time assessments.

title: VideoForge
fullTitle:
VideoForge: Enterprise Video Content Intelligence and
            Searchable Archive Platform
description:
An enterprise video intelligence platform that
            ingests all corporate video content (training videos, sales calls, product demos, conferences, customer
            testimonials), performs multi-modal analysis (speech transcription, speaker identification, scene analysis,
            emotion detection), creates a fully searchable video knowledge base, and auto-generates derivative content
            (clips, summaries, translated versions, highlight reels)—turning video archives from storage costs into
            searchable revenue-generating assets.

detailedDescription.problem: Enterprises produce 10,000-100,000 hours of video content annually (Zoom calls, training, marketing, events) that are immediately archived and never accessed again. Sales calls contain critical coaching opportunities that managers never review. Training videos are rewatched at 0.3x the rate of searchable text documents. Finding a specific moment in 10,000 hours of archive requires watching every minute of every video.
detailedDescription.solution: An enterprise video intelligence platform that ingests all corporate video content (training videos, sales calls, product demos, conferences, customer testimonials), performs multi-modal analysis (speech transcription, speaker identification, scene analysis, emotion detection), creates a fully searchable video knowledge base, and auto-generates derivative content (clips, summaries, translated versions, highlight reels)—turning video archives from storage costs into searchable revenue-generating assets.
detailedDescription.architecture: Multi-Modal Video Analysis Pipeline: Whisper Large v3 transcription with diarization (pyannote.audio) for high-accuracy speech-to-text with speaker attribution. Scene detection and keyframe extraction at scene boundaries. Visual scene understanding: CLIP embeddings for each keyframe enabling visual semantic search ("find all scenes showing product demo on mobile phone"). Face recognition for speaker identification across video library (with consent framework). Emotion and sentiment analysis per speaker segment: tone classification (confident, uncertain, excited), presentation energy scoring. Action recognition: identify presentation events, screen sharing, whiteboard drawing. Multi-Modal Search Index: All extracted signals stored in a vector database (Qdrant): transcript text embeddings, visual scene embeddings, speaker profiles, detected topics. Unified search interface: "find all sales calls where we discussed pricing with enterprise customers" — searches across transcript, visual context, and metadata simultaneously. Auto-clip generation: from a search query, extracts the most relevant 30-90 second clips from any matching video in the library. Derivative Content Generation: Meeting summary → structured document with decisions/actions. Training video → interactive Q&A quiz auto-generated from content. Sales call → coaching feedback report (talking-to-listening ratio, filler word frequency, key topics covered). Customer testimonial → highlight reel with best quotes auto-selected and edited. Multi-language translation with dubbed audio using TTS.

title: GridSentinel
fullTitle:
GridSentinel: Smart Grid Load Forecasting and Demand Response
            Optimization
description:
An AI platform for electric utilities and grid
            operators that generates 15-minute interval load forecasts at feeder and substation level using weather,
            building IoT data, EV charging patterns, and distributed solar generation, then automatically executes
            demand response programs—dispatching load reduction signals to enrolled industrial and commercial customers
            to prevent grid emergencies and avoid expensive peaking power costs.

detailedDescription.problem: Electric grid management costs US utilities $50B+ annually in peaking capacity that sits idle 95% of the time. A 1°F temperature forecast error in summer causes 1-2% load forecast error—leading to $2-5M per event in unnecessary generator dispatch or emergency purchases. Demand response programs could reduce peak demand by 15-20% but are under-utilized because utility systems cannot predict demand precisely enough to preemptively trigger them.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI platform for electric utilities and grid operators that generates 15-minute interval load forecasts at feeder and substation level using weather, building IoT data, EV charging patterns, and distributed solar generation, then automatically executes demand response programs—dispatching load reduction signals to enrolled industrial and commercial customers to prevent grid emergencies and avoid expensive peaking power costs.
detailedDescription.architecture: Load Forecasting Architecture: Feature sources: NOAA weather API (temperature, humidity, cloud cover, wind speed at all substation locations), AMI smart meter data at 15-minute intervals (1M+ meters), commercial building BMS APIs (HVAC setpoint, occupancy), industrial facility production schedules, EV charging station real-time demand, distributed solar generation forecasts from irradiance models. Forecasting model stack: Transformer-based temporal fusion transformer (TFT) for multi-horizon probabilistic forecasting. Separate models: short-term (1-24h ahead at 15-min intervals), day-ahead (24-72h), and week-ahead. Residual loads modeled separately: baseline consumption, weather-sensitive component, behavioral component. Output: probabilistic forecasts at the 10th, 50th, 90th percentile for each feeder/substation. Demand Response Engine: enrolled customer database with: load curtailment capability (kW), notification-to-curtailment lag time, historical response rate, curtailment cost ($/MWh). When forecast exceeds capacity threshold at any substation: automated event trigger calculates required curtailment, selects optimal customer dispatch portfolio (minimize cost, maximize reliability), sends automated curtailment notifications via API/web portal/SMS. Real-time monitoring of customer response. Utility economics: calculate avoided cost vs. peaking capacity using locational marginal pricing data.

title: LegalDiscover
fullTitle:
LegalDiscover: AI-Powered eDiscovery Document Review and
            Privilege Detection
description:
An eDiscovery platform that ingests millions of
            documents in litigation matters, uses predictive coding to identify responsive and privileged documents at
            95%+ accuracy vs. 65-70% for manual review, automatically clusters issues and hot documents, generates
            privilege logs, and reduces document review cost from $0.07-$1.00/document (manual attorney review) to
            $0.002-$0.01/document.

detailedDescription.problem: US eDiscovery is a $16.5B industry with 60-70% of costs consumed by attorney document review at $300-$600/hour. A mid-size litigation matter with 500,000 documents costs $200,000-$800,000 for first-pass review. Predictive coding (TAR) is legally accepted but complex to implement—most firms lack the technical capability to deploy it effectively, defaulting to expensive manual review.
detailedDescription.solution: An eDiscovery platform that ingests millions of documents in litigation matters, uses predictive coding to identify responsive and privileged documents at 95%+ accuracy vs. 65-70% for manual review, automatically clusters issues and hot documents, generates privilege logs, and reduces document review cost from $0.07-$1.00/document (manual attorney review) to $0.002-$0.01/document.
detailedDescription.architecture: Document Ingestion Pipeline: Accepts all common eDiscovery formats: EDRM XML, Relativity export, native files (Office, PDF, email PST/MSG). Text extraction: PDFMiner for PDFs, python-docx for Word, EML parser for emails, OCR (Tesseract + ABBYY cloud for handwritten) for scanned documents. Metadata preservation: custodian, date, document family relationships, email threads. Technology Assisted Review (TAR 2.0 / Continuous Active Learning): seed set of attorney-reviewed documents (100-500 documents sufficient to bootstrap). Active learning loop: model identifies highest-uncertainty documents for attorney review, continuously retrains on new labels. Gradient boosted classifier with text TF-IDF + semantic embeddings (legal domain fine-tuned BERT). Achieves >95% recall at 72% precision—vastly superior to the legal defensibility threshold. Issue coding: multi-label classification for 10-20 user-defined issues per matter. Privilege Detection: Separate multi-class classifier for: attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine, common interest doctrine, non-privileged. Privilege log auto-generation: generates privilege log entries in court-required format from detected privileged documents—describes document, identifies privilege basis, lists privilege holders. Hot document identification: cluster analysis identifies document groups most central to case issues. Conceptual search and timeline visualization for case development.

title: ScoreEngine
fullTitle:
ScoreEngine: Real-Time Alternative Credit Scoring for Thin-File
            Borrowers
description:
A credit scoring API that generates accurate
            creditworthiness assessments for the 45M+ Americans with thin or no credit files, using alternative data
            sources (bank transaction patterns, rental payment history, utility payments, telecom history, gig economy
            income analysis) and ML models that achieve FICO-equivalent predictive accuracy while complying with FCRA
            and ECOA fair lending requirements.

detailedDescription.problem: 45M Americans are 'credit invisible' (no credit file) or 'unscorable' (insufficient history for traditional FICO). These individuals pay 2-4x higher interest rates, cannot access mortgages, and are denied rental housing—a $200B market opportunity. Traditional banks leave this market because they can't assess risk without FICO scores. Community banks, CDFIs, and fintechs serving this population need accurate risk assessment tools.
detailedDescription.solution: A credit scoring API that generates accurate creditworthiness assessments for the 45M+ Americans with thin or no credit files, using alternative data sources (bank transaction patterns, rental payment history, utility payments, telecom history, gig economy income analysis) and ML models that achieve FICO-equivalent predictive accuracy while complying with FCRA and ECOA fair lending requirements.
detailedDescription.architecture: Alternative Data Integration: Bank transaction analysis (with Plaid/Finicity open banking API): monthly cash flow stability, income verification and volatility, rent/utility payment extraction from transactions, savings behavior patterns, spending-to-income ratios. Rental payment history via RentBureau, Experian RentBureau, and rental platform APIs. Telecom and utility payment history via eBureau, IMS data sources. Gig economy income: Argyle API for Uber/Lyft/DoorDash/Instacart earnings verification with volatility scoring. ML Model Architecture: gradient boosting classifier on 200+ engineered features. Key model challenges: lack of longitudinal outcome data requires transfer learning from FICO-scored population. Adversarial debiasing: fairness constraints (equalized odds, demographic parity) applied during training to ensure ECOA compliance across protected classes. Disparate impact testing per Regulation B standards—automated reporting. Calibrated probability output that maps to standard risk tier categories. FCRA compliance: firm offer of credit language, adverse action notice generation, dispute mechanism. Model Governance: automated disparate impact testing monthly. Model explainability per consumer (SHAP values translated to consumer-facing adverse action reasons). Regulatory examination ready documentation: model card, validation report, fair lending analysis.

title: ClinicalTrialAI
fullTitle:
ClinicalTrialAI: Automated Patient Cohort Identification and
            Trial Matching Platform
description:
An AI platform that reads clinical trial
            eligibility criteria in natural language, queries EHR databases for matching patients using multi-criteria
            NLP matching, identifies eligible patients in real-time as their clinical data is updated, and notifies care
            teams of trial opportunities—accelerating clinical trial enrollment by 60% and reducing screen failure rates
            from 40% to under 15%.

detailedDescription.problem: Clinical trial enrollment is the #1 bottleneck in drug development. 85% of trials fail to meet enrollment targets on time; average trial enrollment takes 30% longer than planned, adding $600K-$8M in costs per month of delay. The average Phase III oncology trial costs $11M in delays. 86% of eligible patients are never informed about relevant trials; physicians identify only 2-5% of eligible patients through manual chart review.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI platform that reads clinical trial eligibility criteria in natural language, queries EHR databases for matching patients using multi-criteria NLP matching, identifies eligible patients in real-time as their clinical data is updated, and notifies care teams of trial opportunities—accelerating clinical trial enrollment by 60% and reducing screen failure rates from 40% to under 15%.
detailedDescription.architecture: Eligibility Criteria Parsing: Clinical trials specify inclusion/exclusion criteria in semi-structured natural language: "Age 18-75 years; Confirmed diagnosis of HER2+ breast cancer (IHC 3+ or FISH amplified); ECOG performance status 0-2; No prior anthracycline therapy; Adequate hepatic function (AST/ALT ≤ 2.5× ULN)." NLP pipeline using medically fine-tuned BERT (BioBERT/ClinicalBERT) extracts structured eligibility logic: phenotype criteria, lab value thresholds, temporal constraints, exclusion diagnoses, medication contraindications. Converts criteria to executable FHIR CQL (Clinical Quality Language) queries. Real-Time Patient Matching: FHIR R4 integration with hospital EHR (Epic, Cerner). Continuous monitoring: every 4 hours, new/updated patient records are checked against all active trial criteria. Multi-hop reasoning: "patient has diagnosis X AND had lab value Y within past 30 days AND is NOT on medication Z"—executed as FHIR CQL query against patient data. Probabilistic matching: accounts for incomplete data—if lab value is 3 months old, flags as "possibly eligible, needs recent lab to confirm." Notification and Workflow: care team alert with: patient identifier, trial name, match reason, required confirmatory steps. Integration with EHR workflow as Best Practice Advisory (BPA) alert. Trial sponsor API: reports aggregate enrollment metrics without PHI. Screen failure analysis: after trial completion, analyzes screen failure reasons to improve future matching logic.

title: VoiceGuard
fullTitle:
VoiceGuard: Real-Time Audio Deepfake and Voice Fraud Detection
            Platform
description:
A real-time audio stream analysis API that detects
            AI-synthesized voice content (deepfakes) and voice cloning attacks with 99%+ accuracy and <50ms latency,
              designed for financial institution call centers, government identity verification, and media authenticity
              verification—preventing the $3B annual fraud enabled by voice synthesis attacks.

detailedDescription.problem: Voice deepfake fraud is growing at 300% annually. CEO fraud via synthetic voice cloned from public recordings has stolen $500M+ in documented cases. Bank call center identity verification via voice is circumvented by real-time voice conversion AI. Broadcast media cannot verify whether recorded audio is authentic. Existing detection methods have 10-15% false positive rates that make them commercially unviable.
detailedDescription.solution: A real-time audio stream analysis API that detects AI-synthesized voice content (deepfakes) and voice cloning attacks with 99%+ accuracy and <50ms latency, designed for financial institution call centers, government identity verification, and media authenticity verification—preventing the $3B annual fraud enabled by voice synthesis attacks.
detailedDescription.architecture: Detection Architecture: Multi-feature voice analysis at the physiological level that synthetic voices cannot perfectly replicate: Prosodic analysis: pitch trajectory, speaking rate, pause patterns, stress placement—synthetic voices have characteristic prosodic artifacts. Spectral fingerprinting: mel-spectrogram analysis for artifacts from vocoder (WaveNet, HiFi-GAN, VITS) synthesis pipelines. GAN discriminator adapted for audio: RawNet2 architecture processes raw waveform to detect synthesis artifacts. Glottal pulse analysis: extracts vocal tract characteristics from cepstral coefficients—synthetic voices have inconsistent glottal excitation patterns. Microphone/recording environment analysis: real recordings carry environmental noise signatures that are absent or inconsistent in cloned audio. Ensemble of 5 independent detection models (diverse architectures) with meta-stacker. Real-Time API Design: WebSocket endpoint accepting live audio stream chunks (100ms windows). Per-chunk classification with rolling confidence update. Alert when detection confidence exceeds threshold: immediate flag with synthesis type classification (TTS vs. voice conversion vs. partial manipulation). Sub-50ms end-to-end latency for real-time call center integration. Continuous model updating: new synthesis techniques (new TTS model releases) are detected by monitoring AI voice generation research and proactively adding adversarial training examples. Red team protocol: quarterly internal adversarial testing with latest synthesis tools.

title: InfraGen
fullTitle:
InfraGen: AI-Powered Infrastructure-as-Code Generator and Cloud
            Cost Optimizer
description:
A platform that generates production-ready
            Terraform, Pulumi, and AWS CDK infrastructure code from natural language descriptions of application
            requirements, automatically applies security best practices and cost optimization patterns, estimates
            monthly cloud costs before deployment, and continuously monitors deployed infrastructure to identify idle
            resources, oversized instances, and cost reduction opportunities—reducing cloud waste by 25-40%.

detailedDescription.problem: Organizations waste 32% of their cloud spend on idle or oversized resources ($26B annually globally). Provisioning cloud infrastructure requires specialized DevOps/cloud engineering skills that 70% of development teams lack. Security misconfigurations in cloud infrastructure cause 80% of cloud-related data breaches. Writing production-grade Terraform with security controls and cost optimizations takes DevOps engineers 2-8 hours per infrastructure component.
detailedDescription.solution: A platform that generates production-ready Terraform, Pulumi, and AWS CDK infrastructure code from natural language descriptions of application requirements, automatically applies security best practices and cost optimization patterns, estimates monthly cloud costs before deployment, and continuously monitors deployed infrastructure to identify idle resources, oversized instances, and cost reduction opportunities—reducing cloud waste by 25-40%.
detailedDescription.architecture: IaC Generation Engine: Accepts natural language descriptions: "I need a highly available Node.js API with a PostgreSQL database, auto-scaling, and Redis cache. Expected 500 concurrent users. Must be PCI-DSS compliant." Structured requirement extraction: compute (CPU/memory requirements, expected concurrency), storage (data size, durability requirements), networking (public/private, CDN needs), security (compliance frameworks required), regional requirements. Architecture selection: maps requirements to reference architectures from an internal library of 200+ validated cloud architecture patterns. Terraform code generation: GPT-4o with Terraform-specific fine-tuning generates modular Terraform code with: proper state management (S3 backend + DynamoDB locking), security group rules following least-privilege, KMS encryption for data at rest, CloudTrail logging, IAM roles with minimal permissions, auto-scaling configuration, multi-AZ deployment for high availability. Security hardening: automated application of CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark controls. tfsec static analysis for security issues before output. Cost Estimation: Infracost integration provides accurate monthly cost estimates for every generated configuration before deployment. Right-sizing recommendations based on workload description. Cost Monitoring (Post-Deployment): AWS Cost Explorer API integration for deployed infrastructure analysis. Idle resource detection: EC2 instances with <5% CPU over 7 days, unattached EBS volumes, unused Elastic IPs, over-provisioned RDS instances. Automatic resize recommendations with expected savings.

title: BrandSentinel
fullTitle:
BrandSentinel: Real-Time Brand Reputation Monitoring and Crisis
            Response Platform
description:
A brand intelligence platform that monitors 150M+
            online sources in real-time (news, social media, forums, review sites, regulatory databases), applies NLP to
            detect reputation threats, measures brand sentiment with statistical significance, predicts which negative
            events will escalate into brand crises using early-signal models, and generates crisis response
            playbooks—giving communications teams a 24-48 hour warning window before brand damage is irreversible.

detailedDescription.problem: Brand crises cost companies an average of $537M in market cap per event and take 3.5 years to fully recover. Yet 89% of companies only learn about reputation crises after they've gone viral—when 50,000+ people have already seen negative content. A 24-hour early warning would enable proactive response vs. reactive damage control, with studies showing proactive response reduces crisis impact by 60-80%.
detailedDescription.solution: A brand intelligence platform that monitors 150M+ online sources in real-time (news, social media, forums, review sites, regulatory databases), applies NLP to detect reputation threats, measures brand sentiment with statistical significance, predicts which negative events will escalate into brand crises using early-signal models, and generates crisis response playbooks—giving communications teams a 24-48 hour warning window before brand damage is irreversible.
detailedDescription.architecture: Data Collection at Scale: News: 50,000+ news sources via Google News API, NewsAPI, custom RSS aggregation (English + 40 foreign languages with translation). Social Media: Twitter/X Firehose filtered stream, Reddit API (all subreddits), LinkedIn public posts, TikTok public video description text, Instagram public captions. Review Platforms: G2, Glassdoor, Trustpilot, Yelp, Google Reviews via scraping + official APIs. Forums: Reddit, Quora, niche industry forums, 4chan public boards. Regulatory: SEC enforcement actions, CPSC product safety database, FTC enforcement, FDA safety alerts. Brand Entity Recognition: custom NER model trained on brand mentions including: company name variations, product names, executive names, brand hashtags, misspellings. Disambiguation from unrelated uses. Negative Signal Classification: multi-label classifier for threat types: product safety issue, executive misconduct, data breach, regulatory action, employee crisis, social controversy, competitive attack. Severity scoring: volume trajectory, source authority, sentiment intensity, engagement rate. Crisis Escalation Prediction: time-series model (LSTM) trained on 500+ historical brand crises. Input: current negative signal volume, growth rate, source diversity, topic characteristics. Output: probability this event escalates to major crisis in next 24/48/72 hours. Crisis Response Playbook Generation: for each detected threat type, LLM generates customized response playbook: internal stakeholder notification chain, holding statement template, social media response timeline, media inquiry response guidance, executive talking points.

title: NeuroPrice
fullTitle: NeuroPrice: AI Dynamic Pricing Engine for E-Commerce and Retail
description:
A reinforcement learning-powered dynamic pricing
            platform that continuously optimizes prices across a retailer's entire SKU catalog by learning demand
            elasticity, monitoring competitor pricing, accounting for inventory levels, and maximizing revenue—updating
            prices 2-24 times daily, delivering 5-15% revenue uplift without additional traffic.

detailedDescription.problem: Retail pricing is one of the highest-leverage, most under-optimized business functions. Static pricing leaves 8-15% of potential revenue on the table. Reactive pricing (manual competitor matching) captures only part of the opportunity. Amazon changes prices 2.5M times per day; traditional retailers change prices once per season. The gap in pricing sophistication between Amazon and traditional retailers directly contributes to Amazon's 40%+ US e-commerce market share.
detailedDescription.solution: A reinforcement learning-powered dynamic pricing platform that continuously optimizes prices across a retailer's entire SKU catalog by learning demand elasticity, monitoring competitor pricing, accounting for inventory levels, and maximizing revenue—updating prices 2-24 times daily, delivering 5-15% revenue uplift without additional traffic.
detailedDescription.architecture: Demand Modeling: Per-SKU demand elasticity estimation using historical sales data, cross-price elasticity with substitutes and complements, promotional lift modeling, and time-of-day/week demand patterns. Bayesian demand model provides uncertainty estimates—critical for RL exploration. Competitor Intelligence: automated competitor price monitoring via price intelligence scraping (across 200+ competing retailers, updated every 15 minutes for top 20% of SKUs, hourly for remainder). Competitive positioning score per SKU against monitored competitors. RL Pricing Policy: Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) agent per product category. State: current price, competitor prices, inventory level, demand forecast, time features, margin floor. Action: price adjustment (±0.5%, ±1%, ±2%, ±5%, hold). Reward: revenue generated in next 24 hours. Constraints: minimum margin floor, maximum price change per period (prevents customer perception issues), MAP (Minimum Advertised Price) compliance. Multi-SKU portfolio optimization: prevents cannibalizing sales from complementary SKUs. Safe RL: conservative action selection near margin floors; required human approval for price changes >10% in single step. A/B testing framework: 5% of traffic always in control group at previous prices for causal measurement of pricing impact. Revenue attribution: precise dollar value of RL pricing decisions vs. baseline.

title: SpeechCoach
fullTitle:
SpeechCoach: AI Real-Time Presentation and Communication Skills
            Training Platform
description:
An AI presentation coach that analyzes live or
            recorded speeches using speech analysis, prosody modeling, NLP sentiment and clarity measurement, and
            audience engagement signals, then provides real-time coaching through an earpiece or post-session feedback
            with specific, actionable improvement recommendations—quantifying communication skills and tracking
            improvement over time for sales teams, executives, and public speakers.

detailedDescription.problem: Effective communication is the #1 soft skill correlated with career advancement, yet 73% of Americans fear public speaking. Sales teams with strong presentation skills convert at 3x the rate of weak presenters. Executive communication coaching costs $500-$5,000 per session from human coaches who are subjective, unavailable at scale, and cannot provide real-time feedback during high-stakes presentations.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI presentation coach that analyzes live or recorded speeches using speech analysis, prosody modeling, NLP sentiment and clarity measurement, and audience engagement signals, then provides real-time coaching through an earpiece or post-session feedback with specific, actionable improvement recommendations—quantifying communication skills and tracking improvement over time for sales teams, executives, and public speakers.
detailedDescription.architecture: Real-Time Audio Analysis Pipeline: Whisper streaming transcription for low-latency (300ms) speech-to-text. Prosody feature extraction at 100ms intervals: pitch F0 contour, speaking rate (syllables/second), energy (loudness), voice quality (jitter, shimmer, HNR). Filler word detection: custom token classifier for "um," "uh," "like," "you know," "basically," "literally" with frequency and distribution analysis. Vocabulary assessment: lexical diversity (type-token ratio), technical jargon density for audience calibration, sentence complexity (Flesch-Kincaid readability). Engagement model: audience engagement prediction from speaker energy, variety, and pacing using a model trained on TED Talk engagement ratings. Confidence scoring: trained on audio samples labeled by human raters for perceived confidence; features include pitch variation, speaking rate, pause patterns, volume consistency. Real-Time Feedback Modes: Earpiece mode: gentle audio cues during live presentations (slow down tone, reminder to pause, volume alert). On-screen mode: live dashboard showing current metrics with green/yellow/red indicators. Post-Session Report: PDF coaching report with: timestamped events (filler words, speaking rate spikes, volume drops, long pauses), comparison to session baseline and historical benchmarks, specific improvement recommendations with before/after audio clip examples, progress tracking vs. previous sessions. Team Analytics: sales team communication skills dashboard for managers: performance distribution, improvement trends, correlation with sales outcomes.

title: ArcheoSearch
fullTitle:
ArcheoSearch: AI Archaeological Site Discovery from
            Multispectral Remote Sensing
description:
An AI platform that systematically analyzes
            multispectral satellite imagery, LiDAR elevation data, and soil composition remote sensing to identify
            archaeological site signatures—subsurface structures, crop marks, soil anomalies, ancient road
            networks—automating the survey phase of archaeology and enabling systematic scanning of thousands of square
            kilometers in days rather than decades.

detailedDescription.problem: Archaeological survey is conducted at 0.1-1% of global landmass; 99%+ of ancient history remains unexcavated. Development projects destroy an estimated 50,000+ unrecorded archaeological sites annually before they can be documented. Traditional field survey costs $5,000-$50,000 per square kilometer and takes weeks per site. Remote sensing analysis requires rare specialist skills combining archaeology, geospatial analysis, and data science.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI platform that systematically analyzes multispectral satellite imagery, LiDAR elevation data, and soil composition remote sensing to identify archaeological site signatures—subsurface structures, crop marks, soil anomalies, ancient road networks—automating the survey phase of archaeology and enabling systematic scanning of thousands of square kilometers in days rather than decades.
detailedDescription.architecture: Multi-Source Remote Sensing Fusion: Sentinel-2 multispectral (B2-B12, 10m resolution): crop mark analysis via NDVI anomaly mapping (subsurface structures affect vegetation growth), soil color variation indices for disturbed soil signatures, thermal infrared for subsurface void detection. LiDAR Digital Terrain Models: micro-topography analysis for subtle earthworks (barrows, enclosures, field systems), hillshade visualization with multi-directional illumination, Topographic Position Index for landscape context. Airborne Magnetic Survey data (where available): magnetometry anomaly analysis for buried pits and kilns. Historical Aerial Photography: change detection against 1940s-1950s RAF/USAAF aerial photo archives. Archaeological Signature Detection Models: CNN models trained on 10,000+ known archaeological site signatures per region. Detection categories: enclosures/hillforts, burial mounds (barrows/tumuli), field systems (lynchets, ridge and furrow), Roman roads (linear crop marks), settlement sites (structural parch marks), industrial sites (slag heaps, field kilns). Anomaly ranking system: probability score per detected anomaly with supporting evidence type. Site Report Generation: for each high-confidence detection, generates automated site record in Heritage Environment Record format: grid reference, site type, dimensions, confidence score, supporting evidence (imagery, LiDAR, historical). Client Applications: CRM (Cultural Resource Management) companies, government heritage agencies, development planning departments, universities.

title: FlowCheck
fullTitle:
FlowCheck: Automated API Contract Testing and Regression
            Intelligence Platform
description:
An intelligent API testing platform that infers
            test cases from API traffic patterns (not just OpenAPI specs), generates adversarial test inputs targeting
            boundary conditions and edge cases, detects behavioral regressions by comparing API responses across
            versions, and continuously monitors production API health—replacing thousands of hand-written API tests with
            AI-generated tests that catch 90%+ of regressions.

detailedDescription.problem: API testing is the fastest-growing quality engineering challenge: the average enterprise has 600+ internal and external APIs, each requiring hundreds of test cases. Manual API test writing costs $50-$200 per test case in developer time; comprehensive coverage of 600 APIs requires 60,000-120,000 test cases. 71% of production API incidents are caused by undetected behavioral changes—not missing features, but subtle response format or semantic changes that slip through manual tests.
detailedDescription.solution: An intelligent API testing platform that infers test cases from API traffic patterns (not just OpenAPI specs), generates adversarial test inputs targeting boundary conditions and edge cases, detects behavioral regressions by comparing API responses across versions, and continuously monitors production API health—replacing thousands of hand-written API tests with AI-generated tests that catch 90%+ of regressions.
detailedDescription.architecture: Traffic-Based Test Inference: Deploys as a proxy or sidecar that observes real API traffic (request/response pairs). Extracts behavioral model from traffic: valid input patterns per endpoint, response schema variations, correlation between request parameters and response patterns, latency distributions, error rate patterns. From observed patterns, infers: required vs. optional fields, valid value ranges, enum values in use, dependent field relationships. Generates test cases from inferred behavioral model—no OpenAPI spec required (though specs improve quality if provided). Adversarial Test Generation: For each endpoint, generates: boundary value tests (null, empty string, max length+1, negative numbers, SQL injection, XSS), type confusion tests (string where integer expected), missing required field tests, invalid enum values, extremely large payloads, deeply nested objects. Uses mutation testing on observed request samples to generate edge cases the API might not handle. Regression Detection Engine: Schema diff: detect when response schema changes between versions. Semantic diff: for JSON responses, use semantic similarity to detect when response content changes meaning despite passing schema validation. Behavioral diff: detect changes in which conditions produce 4xx vs. 5xx vs. 2xx responses. Statistical drift: detect when response latency distribution shifts (performance regression). CI/CD Integration: runs generated test suite on every deployment, identifies which tests fail, maps failures to specific code changes (via git blame correlation), generates regression report with severity classification.

title: LanguageBridge
fullTitle:
LanguageBridge: Real-Time Neural Machine Translation with
            Domain Specialization
description:
A domain-specialized neural machine translation API
            that achieves 15-30% better translation quality than Google Translate/DeepL for specific domains (legal,
            medical, technical, financial) by fine-tuning on domain-specific corpora, maintains glossaries for
            brand/product terms, preserves formatting in complex documents, and provides confidence scoring and human
            review routing for low-confidence segments.

detailedDescription.problem: Generic MT systems fail at domain-specific terminology: a financial translation where 'security' means a financial instrument vs. data security, or a medical translation where 'impression' is a clinical finding—these errors are costly and embarrassing. Global enterprises spend $50B annually on translation services; 40% of this is for specialized technical or legal content where generic MT quality is unacceptable.
detailedDescription.solution: A domain-specialized neural machine translation API that achieves 15-30% better translation quality than Google Translate/DeepL for specific domains (legal, medical, technical, financial) by fine-tuning on domain-specific corpora, maintains glossaries for brand/product terms, preserves formatting in complex documents, and provides confidence scoring and human review routing for low-confidence segments.
detailedDescription.architecture: Domain-Specific MT Architecture: Base model: multilingual M2M-100 (supports 100 languages, 10,000+ language pairs). Domain fine-tuning: supervised fine-tuning on domain-specific parallel corpora: Legal: MultiUN, JRC-Acquis, EURLOC (3M+ sentence pairs); Medical: Medline abstracts, clinical trial translations, WHO document corpus; Financial: SEC EDGAR international filings, financial news parallel corpus; Technical: patent translations from WIPO, technical manual corpus. QLoRA fine-tuning: parameter-efficient fine-tuning enabling domain models per language pair without full model retraining. Terminology Consistency Engine: client-specific glossary enforcement using constrained decoding (lexical forcing): force translation of approved terms regardless of what base model prefers. Terminology extraction from client documents automatically builds glossary candidates for human approval. Consistency checker flags when same source term is translated differently in same document. Document Format Preservation: DOCX: paragraph-level translation preserving styles, tables, headers. PPTX: slide-by-slide translation preserving layout. PDF: text extraction → translation → PDF regeneration with original formatting reconstruction. HTML: tag-preserving translation. Quality Estimation and Routing: Per-segment quality estimation model (TransQuest or CometKiwi) predicts translation quality without reference translation. Low-confidence segments (<0.6 QE score) flagged for human post-editing. Integration with CAT tools (SDL Trados, memoQ, Phrase) for human post-editing workflow.

title: WasteOptimizer
fullTitle:
WasteOptimizer: AI Municipal Solid Waste Route Optimization and
            Fill-Level Prediction
description:
A smart waste management platform that deploys
            ultrasonic fill-level sensors in waste bins, predicts when each bin will be full using ML, dynamically
            optimizes collection routes to only collect full bins, reduces collection vehicle fuel consumption by
            30-40%, and prevents overflow events—delivering smart city waste management at 10% of the cost of
            proprietary solutions.

detailedDescription.problem: Municipal waste collection is one of the largest urban operating budgets—$20B annually in the US, 60-70% of which is fuel and labor. Traditional fixed-route collection picks up bins that are 30% full (waste of fuel) and misses bins that overflow before collection (public health issue). Smart bin sensor systems from proprietary vendors cost $150-$400/bin, prohibitive for cities with 10,000-100,000 bins.
detailedDescription.solution: A smart waste management platform that deploys ultrasonic fill-level sensors in waste bins, predicts when each bin will be full using ML, dynamically optimizes collection routes to only collect full bins, reduces collection vehicle fuel consumption by 30-40%, and prevents overflow events—delivering smart city waste management at 10% of the cost of proprietary solutions.
detailedDescription.architecture: Low-Cost IoT Sensor Network: Custom IoT sensor node using: HC-SR04 ultrasonic distance sensor ($1.50) for fill level measurement, ESP32 microcontroller ($3) with LoRaWAN connectivity, solar panel + small battery for maintenance-free operation. Total hardware cost: $15-25/bin vs. $150-400 for proprietary solutions. LoRaWAN network coverage via The Things Network (free) or private LoRa gateway deployment for city coverage. Fill-Level Prediction Model: Per-bin LSTM model trained on fill-level time series. Features: day of week, local events calendar, nearby business hours, weather (precipitation increases waste from outdoor dining), holiday effects, historical fill patterns. Prediction horizon: 24-72 hours. Dynamic Route Optimization: Daily: generate optimal collection routes visiting only bins predicted to be >70% full by EOD, using OR-Tools VRP solver with time window constraints. Real-time updates: if sensor shows unexpected fill spike (event, storm, malfunction), dynamically re-route nearby vehicle. Fleet management: dispatch right vehicle size based on total collection volume. Overflow Prevention: Predictive alerts 12 hours before predicted overflow for high-traffic bins requiring more frequent collection or capacity upgrade. Platform Delivery: City operations center dashboard: real-time bin fill map, fleet tracking, route efficiency metrics, overflow alert management. ROI: 30-40% fuel savings, 15-20% labor hour savings, near-elimination of overflow complaints.

title: InvoiceAI
fullTitle:
InvoiceAI: Intelligent Invoice Processing and Accounts Payable
            Automation Platform
description:
An accounts payable AI platform that processes any
            invoice format (PDF, image, XML, EDI) using multi-model document understanding, validates against PO and
            contract terms, detects duplicate invoices and overbilling, routes for approval based on business rules,
            initiates payment, and reconciles to the general ledger—compressing 15-day invoice cycle times to 2 days and
            reducing AP processing cost from $15-$50/invoice to $1-$3.

detailedDescription.problem: US companies process 30 billion invoices annually at an average manual cost of $15-$50 each—a $450B-$1.5T annual expense. Average invoice processing takes 15-45 days, causing missed early payment discounts (2/10 net 30 = 36% annualized discount). AP teams catch only 30% of duplicate invoices and overbilling; invoice fraud costs companies $2.4B annually. A 50-person company with $5M in annual payables processes 500+ invoices monthly at $10,000-$25,000 in monthly processing costs.
detailedDescription.solution: An accounts payable AI platform that processes any invoice format (PDF, image, XML, EDI) using multi-model document understanding, validates against PO and contract terms, detects duplicate invoices and overbilling, routes for approval based on business rules, initiates payment, and reconciles to the general ledger—compressing 15-day invoice cycle times to 2 days and reducing AP processing cost from $15-$50/invoice to $1-$3.
detailedDescription.architecture: Multi-Format Invoice Ingestion: Email attachment monitoring via IMAP/MS Graph API. Vendor portal upload. EDI 810 electronic invoice parsing. PDF/image OCR using LayoutLMv3 + Donut (document understanding transformer) for high-accuracy field extraction regardless of invoice format/layout: vendor name, invoice number, date, line items, quantities, unit prices, tax, total. Zero-shot generalization: works on unseen invoice layouts without template configuration—unlike template-based systems. Validation Engine: Three-way match: invoice vs. PO vs. goods receipt. Quantity match: invoiced quantity matches PO and receiving record. Price match: invoiced unit price matches contracted price (tolerance configurable). Terms validation: payment terms match vendor master. Duplicate detection: fuzzy matching on invoice number + vendor + amount + date combination. Overbilling detection: compares aggregated year-to-date invoices against contract value. Approval Workflow: configurable routing rules: amount-based (invoices >$10K route to CFO), GL account-based, exception-based (price variances flag for review). Mobile approval app for managers. SLA tracking: alert escalation if approval exceeds configured turnaround. Payment Initiation: ERP integration (SAP, Oracle, NetSuite) for payment file generation. Dynamic discounting: automatically capture early payment discounts when working capital permits. Reconciliation: auto-post to GL using AI-suggested GL coding from vendor category and historical patterns.

title: TumorScope
fullTitle:
TumorScope: AI Multi-Modal Radiology Report Generation and
            Lesion Tracking
description:
An AI radiology assistant that analyzes CT, MRI,
            and PET scans using multi-modal deep learning, detects and characterizes lesions, generates structured
            radiology reports in radiologist's natural language style, tracks lesion changes over time against prior
            studies, and alerts to new or growing lesions—reducing radiologist report generation time from 20-30 minutes
            to 5-8 minutes while improving detection of subtle findings.

detailedDescription.problem: US radiologists face 37% productivity shortfall: demand for radiology readings exceeds radiologist capacity by 30M+ studies/year. Average radiologist reads 20,000+ studies per year with 8-12 hour days, resulting in fatigue-driven diagnostic errors. Radiology errors (missed findings, mis-characterized lesions) contribute to 28% of diagnostic errors, costing $1.1B in malpractice claims. Reports take 20-30 minutes; AI-assisted reports could cut this to 5-8 minutes.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI radiology assistant that analyzes CT, MRI, and PET scans using multi-modal deep learning, detects and characterizes lesions, generates structured radiology reports in radiologist's natural language style, tracks lesion changes over time against prior studies, and alerts to new or growing lesions—reducing radiologist report generation time from 20-30 minutes to 5-8 minutes while improving detection of subtle findings.
detailedDescription.architecture: Medical Imaging Processing Pipeline: DICOM file ingestion via DICOMweb standard. Multi-modal support: CT (chest, abdomen, pelvis), MRI (brain, spine, MSK), PET-CT. Pre-processing: window/level normalization, orientation standardization, multi-planar reconstruction. Lesion Detection Models: Separate detection models per organ/modality: lung nodule detection (nnU-Net 3D segmentation, trained on NLST and LUNA16 datasets), liver lesion detection (LI-RADS classification model), breast lesion detection (trained on CBIS-DDSM), brain abnormality detection (stroke, tumor, MS lesion). Lesion characterization: morphology features (shape, margins, density/signal intensity), location in standardized anatomical coordinates, volumetric measurement with confidence interval, ACR-RADS scoring (BIRADS, LI-RADS, Lung-RADS, TI-RADS). Report Generation: structured report generation using radiology-fine-tuned LLM (trained on 500,000+ de-identified radiology reports). Produces: technique description, findings section per organ system, impression summary. Style adaptation: learns each radiologist's writing style from their past reports. Impression auto-generation: prioritized clinical findings with recommended follow-up. Prior Study Comparison: automated lesion registration between current and prior studies. Change tracking: volumetric comparison, growth rate calculation (response criteria: RECIST 1.1, iRECIST). Trend line visualization for multifocal disease monitoring.

title: ShipGuard
fullTitle:
ShipGuard: Maritime Vessel Risk Scoring and Insurance Pricing
            Intelligence Platform
description:
A maritime risk intelligence platform for marine
            insurers and P&I clubs that scores each vessel's real-time risk using AIS tracking data (route deviations,
            dark vessel behavior, speed anomalies), port state control inspection history, vessel age/class/maintenance
            records, and environmental route conditions—generating dynamic hull and cargo insurance premiums that
            accurately reflect actual risk exposure.

detailedDescription.problem: Marine insurance is a $35B global market plagued by information asymmetry: underwriters price vessels based on static characteristics (type, age, flag) while real risk is driven by operational behavior (where vessels actually go, how they navigate, maintenance quality). Marine insurance combined ratios consistently exceed 100%, indicating systematic underpricing. AIS data enabling behavioral risk scoring exists but no platform currently uses it for real-time dynamic underwriting.
detailedDescription.solution: A maritime risk intelligence platform for marine insurers and P&I clubs that scores each vessel's real-time risk using AIS tracking data (route deviations, dark vessel behavior, speed anomalies), port state control inspection history, vessel age/class/maintenance records, and environmental route conditions—generating dynamic hull and cargo insurance premiums that accurately reflect actual risk exposure.
detailedDescription.architecture: AIS Data Pipeline: Real-time AIS data stream (class A transponders, 2-second update intervals) for all commercial vessels via MarineTraffic or Spire Maritime API. Historical track reconstruction for 3+ years per vessel. Behavioral Feature Engineering: Route risk scoring: overlay vessel tracks against geopolitical risk zones (Crimea, Strait of Hormuz, Malacca piracy zones, US OFAC sanctioned ports). Dark vessel detection: intervals where vessel goes AIS-dark (transponder disabled)—major fraud/sanctions risk indicator. Speed anomaly: unusual speed profiles indicating mechanical issues or dangerous navigation. Port call analysis: frequency of calls to high-risk ports, time in port vs. historical baseline. Weather risk exposure: historical route overlaid against NOAA storm track database—how often has this vessel transited during extreme weather? Vessel Physical Risk Model: Age and flag state registry quality score. Class society survey history: DNV/Lloyd's/Bureau Veritas inspection outcomes. Port state control deficiency history (Paris MOU, Tokyo MOU inspection database). Casualty history: ICS/IMO casualty database cross-reference. Cargo type risk: hazardous material declarations, container vs. tanker risk profiles. Dynamic Premium Calculation: GLM-based pricing model with behavioral ML risk score as key rating factor. Real-time premium update as vessel behavior changes during policy period. Voyage-specific pricing for one-off charter coverage. Cargo insurance: track container movements, price based on route risk exposure.

title: NeuralCopy
fullTitle:
NeuralCopy: AI Conversion Rate Optimization and Landing Page
            Copywriting Engine
description:
An AI copywriting platform that generates
            high-converting landing page copy, email sequences, and ad copy by learning from 50M+ A/B test results
            across industries, analyzing the target audience's psychology, applying behavioral economics principles, and
            generating multiple variants for systematic testing—delivering copy that outperforms human copywriters at
            10% of the cost and 100x the speed.

detailedDescription.problem: Professional direct response copywriters charge $2,500-$25,000 per sales page; top copywriters are booked 6-12 months in advance. Most businesses settle for average copy that converts at 2-3%; great copy converts at 8-15%—a 3-5x revenue difference on the same traffic. A/B testing to discover winning copy takes months; AI can learn from aggregate A/B test results across thousands of businesses to immediately apply proven patterns.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI copywriting platform that generates high-converting landing page copy, email sequences, and ad copy by learning from 50M+ A/B test results across industries, analyzing the target audience's psychology, applying behavioral economics principles, and generating multiple variants for systematic testing—delivering copy that outperforms human copywriters at 10% of the cost and 100x the speed.
detailedDescription.architecture: Conversion Intelligence Database: Aggregated A/B test results from 50M+ split tests from marketing platform partners: headline variants and their relative conversion impact, CTA button text performance data, social proof placement effects, pricing anchor patterns, guarantee copy variants. Psychological model library: 40+ cognitive bias applications (scarcity, social proof, authority, reciprocity, loss aversion, framing) with proven copy patterns per bias. Audience Psychology Profiling: For each target audience definition, builds a psychographic profile using: demographic-to-psychographic mapping from research databases, stage-of-awareness assessment (unaware/problem-aware/solution-aware/product-aware/most aware), primary buying motivation (gain, pain, fear), objection library from support ticket analysis. Copy Generation Architecture: GPT-4o fine-tuned on 100,000+ high-converting sales pages with conversion rate metadata. Structured generation pipeline: hook → problem agitation → solution → proof → offer → CTA. Each element is independently generated with 3 variants for testing. Variant Differentiation: variants differ on core dimension (emotional vs. logical appeal, short vs. long form, benefit vs. feature-led)—not just word choices. Systematic Testing Framework: statistical power calculation for required sample size, sequential testing with early stopping (SPRT method), multi-armed bandit optimization for faster convergence to winner. Integration with Unbounce, Optimizely, Google Optimize for direct test deployment.

title: GraphMap
fullTitle:
GraphMap: Enterprise Knowledge Graph Construction and
            Organizational Intelligence Platform
description:
An enterprise knowledge graph platform that
            automatically extracts entities and relationships from internal documents, emails, wikis, and meeting notes
            to build a continuously updated organizational knowledge graph—enabling employees to find organizational
            expertise, trace decision provenance, surface relevant past work, and answer complex multi-hop
            organizational questions that no search engine can answer.

detailedDescription.problem: Fortune 500 companies have 70% of their institutional knowledge trapped in unstructured documents, emails, and employees' heads. Knowledge workers spend 19% of their time searching for information and another 14% communicating to find information—$3.5T in global productivity loss. When key employees leave, 50-80% of their role-specific knowledge leaves with them. New employee ramp-up takes 3-6 months because institutional knowledge is non-navigable.
detailedDescription.solution: An enterprise knowledge graph platform that automatically extracts entities and relationships from internal documents, emails, wikis, and meeting notes to build a continuously updated organizational knowledge graph—enabling employees to find organizational expertise, trace decision provenance, surface relevant past work, and answer complex multi-hop organizational questions that no search engine can answer.
detailedDescription.architecture: Knowledge Extraction Pipeline: Ingests: SharePoint/OneDrive documents, Confluence wiki pages, Slack/Teams message archives (with consent), Google Workspace Drive, email archives (with consent), Jira/GitHub issue history. NLP extraction pipeline per document: entity recognition (people, organizations, products, projects, technologies, dates), relation extraction (works on, responsible for, decided, approved, built, presented to), event extraction (product launch, decision made, issue resolved). Temporal knowledge graph: all relationships are time-stamped—knows when X became relevant, when a decision was made, when a project transitioned. Knowledge Graph Construction (Neo4j): node types: Person, Team, Project, Product, Technology, Document, Decision, Customer. edge types: authored, approved, participated_in, depends_on, succeeded, preceded, mentioned_in. Graph embedding (TransE, RotatE) for efficient traversal and similarity search. Query Interface: Natural language to Cypher query translation using LLM. Answers complex multi-hop questions: "Who in the company has experience with both Kubernetes and financial services regulations?" traverses person→skill nodes. "What led to the decision to use PostgreSQL over MongoDB in 2021?" traverses decision→document→meeting nodes. Expertise location: for any technical question, identifies the 3 most knowledgeable people with evidence (documents they authored, issues they resolved). Institutional memory preservation: automatically archives knowledge from departing employees' recent work into the knowledge graph.

title: ContaminantTrack
fullTitle:
ContaminantTrack: Water Quality Monitoring and Contamination
            Source Attribution AI
description:
An AI platform for water utilities and
            environmental agencies that processes real-time sensor data from water distribution networks to detect
            contamination events, trace contamination back to source using hydraulic modeling and ML, predict downstream
            spread, and generate automated regulatory notifications and remediation plans—cutting contamination event
            response time from days to hours.

detailedDescription.problem: Flint, Michigan-style water contamination crises cost cities $500M-$5B and affect public health for years. Water utilities deploy 500-5,000 sensors but have no AI to interpret their readings. Current contamination detection relies on manual lab sampling with 24-72 hour turnaround—creating a critical detection gap. The Bioterrorism Act requires utilities to have early warning systems; most don't truly have one.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI platform for water utilities and environmental agencies that processes real-time sensor data from water distribution networks to detect contamination events, trace contamination back to source using hydraulic modeling and ML, predict downstream spread, and generate automated regulatory notifications and remediation plans—cutting contamination event response time from days to hours.
detailedDescription.architecture: Sensor Network Integration: Connects to water quality sensors: turbidity, pH, ORP (oxidation-reduction potential), chlorine residual, temperature, conductivity, TOC (total organic carbon), nitrate. Real-time data ingestion via SCADA/Modbus protocol. 15-second sampling intervals for continuous monitoring. Contamination Detection Model: Baseline behavioral model: multivariate time-series of all sensor readings under normal conditions (seasonal, time-of-day patterns). Anomaly ensemble: Isolation Forest + LSTM autoencoder for multi-sensor anomaly detection. Multi-sensor correlation analysis: real contamination events produce correlated responses across geographically related sensors; spurious single-sensor failures produce isolated anomalies. Contamination Source Attribution: Hydraulic network model (EPANET Python wrapper) loaded with network topology, pipe properties, and current flow data. Given detected contamination event location and timing, runs backward-tracing analysis: which sources could have caused this observation given travel times? Probability-weighted source candidates. Spread Prediction: Forward EPANET simulation from attributed source: predicts which zones will be affected in next 1-6 hours and at what concentration. Enables proactive advisory issuance before contamination reaches service connections. Response Automation: Regulatory notification: EPA and state authority incident report auto-drafted. Customer advisory: automated "do not drink" alert targeted by service zone. Valve isolation recommendation: identifies hydraulic valves to close to minimize spread. Remediation tracking: monitors recovery as chlorine residuals return to normal.

title: SafetyMatrix
fullTitle:
SafetyMatrix: Construction Site Hazard Detection and Compliance
            Monitoring
description:
A construction site safety platform that processes
            live video feeds from site cameras to detect OSHA safety violations in real-time (missing PPE, fall hazards,
            equipment exclusion zone violations, unsafe work practices), alerts site supervisors within seconds,
            generates OSHA-compliant incident reports, and tracks safety metrics—reducing recordable incident rates by
            50-70% and OSHA fines by 80-90%.

detailedDescription.problem: Construction is the most dangerous industry: 1 in 5 worker deaths in the US occurs in construction (4,764 fatalities in 2020). OSHA fines for serious violations average $15,625 each; citations average $14,000 per inspection. Workers' compensation insurance premiums for construction average 8-15% of payroll—$240B annually. Most violations are detectable visually but require human safety officers who cannot be everywhere simultaneously.
detailedDescription.solution: A construction site safety platform that processes live video feeds from site cameras to detect OSHA safety violations in real-time (missing PPE, fall hazards, equipment exclusion zone violations, unsafe work practices), alerts site supervisors within seconds, generates OSHA-compliant incident reports, and tracks safety metrics—reducing recordable incident rates by 50-70% and OSHA fines by 80-90%.
detailedDescription.architecture: Computer Vision Safety Detection: Camera integration via RTSP streaming from existing site cameras (works with any IP camera, no proprietary hardware required). Real-time inference at 15-30 FPS using YOLOv8 + custom safety detection heads: PPE detection: hard hat (by color per contractor), hi-vis vest, safety glasses, fall protection harness, steel-toed boots—detects absence per person. Fall hazard detection: unguarded edge proximity (worker within 6 feet of unprotected edge above 4 feet). Equipment exclusion zone: configurable exclusion zones around operating heavy equipment; worker intrusion triggers immediate alert. Unsafe work practice: ladder angle, lifting posture, two-person carry violations. Fire extinguisher coverage mapping: ensure required coverage per floor plan. Multi-Camera Management: 50-500 camera streams processed per deployment via GPU cluster. Spatial awareness: zone-aware alerts (different rules for different site areas). Privacy mode: automatic blurring of worker faces in footage shared externally. Alert System: sub-30-second alert from violation detection to supervisor notification. Priority routing: critical (fall/exclusion zone) = immediate radio/phone call; serious (missing PPE) = app notification with photo; advisory = end-of-shift summary. Incident Report Generation: OSHA 300 log auto-update. Inspection-ready report with timestamped photos, location, violation type, frequency. Workers' compensation documentation.

title: ChemSynth
fullTitle:
ChemSynth: AI Retrosynthetic Pathway Planning for Drug
            Discovery
description:
An AI retrosynthesis platform for medicinal
            chemists that takes a target drug molecule SMILES string and generates the most commercially viable
            multi-step synthesis pathways using transformer-based retrosynthetic models, predicts reaction conditions
            and yields, checks commercial availability of starting materials, and estimates synthesis cost—compressing
            2-4 week manual retrosynthetic analysis to 10 minutes.

detailedDescription.problem: Drug synthesis planning (retrosynthesis) requires 10-20 years of expert chemist training and takes 2-4 weeks per complex target molecule. Pharmaceutical companies employ 50-200 synthetic chemists at $120K-$250K/year each for this work. Failed synthesis attempts due to poor pathway selection waste $50K-$200K in reagents and researcher time per failure. The global pharmaceutical R&D market is $238B; synthesis planning is a critical bottleneck.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI retrosynthesis platform for medicinal chemists that takes a target drug molecule SMILES string and generates the most commercially viable multi-step synthesis pathways using transformer-based retrosynthetic models, predicts reaction conditions and yields, checks commercial availability of starting materials, and estimates synthesis cost—compressing 2-4 week manual retrosynthetic analysis to 10 minutes.
detailedDescription.architecture: Retrosynthetic AI Architecture: Base model: AizynthFinder + custom transformer retrosynthesis model trained on USPTO patent reaction database (5M+ reactions). SMILES-based molecular encoding using chemical transformer (MolBERT/ChemBERTa). Single-step retrosynthesis model: given a product SMILES, predicts the most likely retrosynthetic disconnections with reaction template and predicted precursors. Multi-step search: Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) expands retrosynthetic tree to 8-12 steps deep, evaluating path quality at each node. Path Scoring: Commercial availability check: each intermediate and starting material checked against eMolecules/Sigma-Aldrich/Combi-Blocks databases (50M+ purchasable compounds). Starting material cost estimation from Sigma-Aldrich pricing API. Predicted yield per step using reaction condition classification model + yield prediction regression. Overall path score: commercial availability × estimated yield × cost × number of steps. Reaction Condition Prediction: For each proposed reaction step, predicts optimal conditions: solvent, temperature, reaction time, catalyst, protecting group requirements. Based on reaction SMARTS pattern + similar historical reactions from USPTO. Hazard assessment: identifies steps involving hazardous reagents (pyrophoric, explosive, toxic) and suggests safer alternatives. Interface: interactive retrosynthetic tree visualization. Chemist can manually explore branches, prune unfavorable paths, and annotate with notes. Export: reaction scheme as PowerPoint/PDF for team review.

title: DebtFlow
fullTitle:
DebtFlow: AI-Powered Debt Collection Optimization and
            Behavioral Prediction Platform
description:
An AI debt collection platform that segments
            delinquent accounts by propensity-to-pay and payment capacity, predicts optimal contact channel and timing
            for each debtor, auto-generates personalized settlement offers, uses NLP to analyze call transcripts for
            escalation risk, and ensures full FDCPA/TCPA compliance—improving collection rates by 20-35% while reducing
            legal compliance risk to near-zero.

detailedDescription.problem: US consumer debt collections is a $15B industry with average collection rates of 20-30% for charged-off debt. Manual collection agents make undifferentiated outreach—same script, same timing, same offer for all accounts. FDCPA violations cost collectors $5,000-$15,000 per violation in class action exposure. Machine learning could segment accounts to focus effort on the 20% of accounts generating 80% of collectable dollars.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI debt collection platform that segments delinquent accounts by propensity-to-pay and payment capacity, predicts optimal contact channel and timing for each debtor, auto-generates personalized settlement offers, uses NLP to analyze call transcripts for escalation risk, and ensures full FDCPA/TCPA compliance—improving collection rates by 20-35% while reducing legal compliance risk to near-zero.
detailedDescription.architecture: Account Scoring Models: Propensity-to-pay model: gradient boosting on account characteristics (debt amount, age, original creditor type), payment history (any payments in past 90 days), demographic proxies (zip code income level, unemployment rate), credit bureau data (current balance trend, new credit inquiries). Capacity-to-pay model: estimate disposable income from regional income data, known financial obligations. Payment pattern model: LSTM on historical payment sequence to predict whether account will self-cure, respond to settlement, or require legal action. Optimal Outreach Strategy: Per-account recommendation: channel (phone, SMS, email, letter), timing (day of week, time of day by demographic), tone (empathetic, firm, settlement-focused), settlement offer amount (minimum acceptable % of balance). Settlement offer optimization: RL agent trained on historical acceptance rates per offer type and account segment. NLP Call Analysis: Real-time transcription of collection calls via Whisper. FDCPA compliance monitoring: detect prohibited language ("arrest," "jail," excessive contact, third-party disclosure). Escalation detection: identify callers showing distress indicators (legal threat mentions, financial hardship claims)—route to senior agent. Call quality scoring for agent coaching. Compliance Automation: Time-zone compliance: enforce TCPA contact hours automatically. Contact frequency limits per account. Mini-Miranda auto-verification before every call. Dispute flag management: automatic cease communication upon dispute.

title: AutoSpec
fullTitle:
AutoSpec: AI Requirements Engineering and Software
            Specification Generator
description:
An AI platform that transforms informal product
            ideas, business requirements, and user interviews into comprehensive, structured Software Requirements
            Specifications (SRS), user stories, acceptance criteria, API contracts, and system architecture
            diagrams—eliminating the 40-60 hour manual requirements engineering phase and reducing requirements-driven
            development rework by 30-50%.

detailedDescription.problem: Requirements defects cause 40-60% of all software project failures and 70-85% of software rework. A poor requirements document costs $50-$200 per defect to fix in requirements phase vs. $4,000-$16,000 in production. Product managers and business analysts spend 40-60 hours per feature on requirements documentation—work that generates zero direct customer value. 60% of all features built are rarely or never used, largely due to poorly validated requirements.
detailedDescription.solution: An AI platform that transforms informal product ideas, business requirements, and user interviews into comprehensive, structured Software Requirements Specifications (SRS), user stories, acceptance criteria, API contracts, and system architecture diagrams—eliminating the 40-60 hour manual requirements engineering phase and reducing requirements-driven development rework by 30-50%.
detailedDescription.architecture: Requirements Elicitation Processing: Input modes: (1) natural language description, (2) user interview transcript, (3) rough wireframes/mockups (image input), (4) existing system documentation. Requirement extraction using GPT-4o with a structured requirements extraction prompt: identifies functional requirements (what system must do), non-functional requirements (performance, security, scalability, compliance), business rules, data requirements, integration requirements. Requirement quality checking: detects ambiguous language ("the system should be fast"), unmeasurable requirements, missing actor specification, conflicting requirements between sections. IEEE 830 SRS Generation: auto-generates IEEE 830 compliant SRS document: introduction (purpose, scope, definitions), overall description (product perspective, functions, user characteristics, constraints), specific requirements (functional, interface, performance, design constraints). User Story Generation: converts requirements to user story format: As a [user type], I want to [action], So that [benefit]. Acceptance criteria: Gherkin (Given-When-Then) acceptance criteria per story, mapping to BDD test framework. API Contract Generation: from functional requirements, generates OpenAPI 3.0 specification with all endpoints, request/response schemas, authentication requirements, error codes. Architecture Recommendations: system context diagram (C4 Level 1), container diagram showing major components (C4 Level 2), tech stack recommendations with justification, estimated development complexity and team composition.

title: InsightCart
fullTitle:
InsightCart: Retail Customer Behavioral Analytics and
            Personalization Engine
description:
A deep behavioral analytics platform for retail
            e-commerce that models every customer's individual preference state using a transformer-based sequential
            recommendation model, delivers hyper-personalized product recommendations, personalizes site search results,
            and generates individual-level next-best-action suggestions for marketing—delivering Amazon-level
            personalization to any mid-market retailer at $500/month.

detailedDescription.problem: Product recommendation accounts for 35% of Amazon's revenue; Spotify's discovery features drive 31% of streams. Yet 95% of retailers use simple rule-based recommendation engines (bestsellers, collaborative filtering) that treat all customers as similar. A personalized customer experience increases conversion rate by 10-30% and average order value by 15-25%—representing $2-5M in additional annual revenue for a $20M revenue e-commerce brand.
detailedDescription.solution: A deep behavioral analytics platform for retail e-commerce that models every customer's individual preference state using a transformer-based sequential recommendation model, delivers hyper-personalized product recommendations, personalizes site search results, and generates individual-level next-best-action suggestions for marketing—delivering Amazon-level personalization to any mid-market retailer at $500/month.
